**Race Condition Prevention**:
- Multiple hooks may run concurrently
- Use directory-based locking (atomic on most filesystems)
- Timeout on lock acquisition to prevent deadlock
- Non-blocking requirement means we log warning but continue

**Input Validation**:
- Malformed JSON could cause jq to hang or error
- Use jq empty to validate JSON structure first
- Read with timeout to prevent hanging on stdin

**Path Security**:
- File paths may contain spaces, quotes, or shell metacharacters
- Use proper quoting in all path operations
- Validate paths don't contain directory traversal (..)

**Disk Full Scenarios**:
- All writes use >> which will fail gracefully if disk full
- Log rotation prevents unbounded growth
- Non-blocking means we continue even if write fails

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Blocking on I/O or lock acquisition
- ❌ Unbounded log growth without rotation
- ❌ Crashing on malformed input
- ❌ Not handling concurrent access
- ❌ Shell injection via unquoted paths
- ❌ Hanging on stdin read

title: Simplify brainstorming skill for Claude 4.7
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Brainstorming skill is building-only (thinking mode removed; Consider skill handles thinking, out of scope for this epic)
- Heavy architecture loading removed from Step 1 (no LikeC4 MCP dance, no ADR scan, no data flow dispatch; codebase-investigator and internet-researcher remain)
- Step 4 Architecture Impact Check added — 5 structural questions against the designed solution with routing to /intuition
- Design-time friction trigger added — skill detects structural awkwardness and offers /intuition
- Epic template restructured: exactly 6 top-level sections + Design Rationale with 6 subsections
- Design Discovery section deleted from template; Key Decisions Made merged into Design Rationale
- Architecture Context section deleted from template
- Architecture Update Checklist deleted from template (belongs in review-implementation)
- Critical rules: exactly 10 rules
- New rules added: "Offer /intuition when design-time friction detected", "Architecture Impact Check required before epic finalization", "Subsection 'None' requires grounded justification"
- Every template subsection with potential empty state requires "None because [grounded reason]" format; bare "None" is insufficient

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md rewritten with simplified structure
- [ ] No references to "thinking mode", mode detection gate, or Step 0 anywhere in the file
- [ ] Step 1 contains no LikeC4 MCP calls, ADR scanning, or dynamic view loading
- [ ] Step 4 (Architecture Impact Check) present with all 5 structural questions
- [ ] Design-time friction trigger present in Step 3 (Design Presentation)
- [ ] Epic template top-level sections (in order): Requirements, Success Criteria, Anti-Patterns, Approach, Architecture, Architecture Impact, Design Rationale
- [ ] Design Rationale subsections (in order): Problem, Research Findings, Key Decisions Made, Approaches Considered, Scope Boundaries, Open Questions
- [ ] Architecture Update Checklist NOT present in epic template
- [ ] Architecture Context NOT present in epic template
- [ ] Design Discovery NOT present as a separate template section
- [ ] Critical rules section has exactly 10 rules
- [ ] Rules include by exact text: "Offer /intuition when design-time friction detected"
- [ ] Rules include by exact text: "Architecture Impact Check required before epic finalization"
- [ ] Rules include by exact text: "Subsection 'None' requires grounded justification"
- [ ] plugin.json version bumped appropriately (minor bump for refactor)
- [ ] Secondary files audited for stale references (commands/brainstorm.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- Reintroducing thinking mode or mode detection gate (reason: separate /consider skill will handle thinking; this epic is strictly building-only)
- Reintroducing heavy architecture loading in Step 1 — LikeC4 MCP, ADR scanning, dynamic view loading (reason: redundant with intuition's 10-pass analysis; brainstorming routes to intuition, doesn't duplicate it)
- Recreating intuition's structural analysis in brainstorming (reason: the Check is a routing mechanism, not an analysis; duplicating intuition's work is cruft)
- Accepting bare "None" or "N/A" in epic template examples without grounded reason (reason: enables skip-thinking rationalization; conversation itself demonstrated this failure mode)
- Adding new sections to epic template beyond the agreed 6 + Design Rationale (reason: bloat we're specifically cutting; any addition requires explicit design review)
- Making SRE refinement optional (reason: framework strength, hard-mandatory; softening on "feels heavy" grounds is the exact rationalization hard rules prevent — flagged explicitly in this conversation)
- Making Architecture Impact Check optional (reason: routing to /intuition only works if required; optional means skipped under pressure)
- Preemptively creating /consider skill in this epic (reason: scope boundary; separate epic handles thinking skill)
- Preemptively updating executor agent for mid-build friction escape hatch (reason: scope boundary; separate future work)

## Approach
Rewrite skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md from scratch following the agreed structure. Delete the thinking-mode path and mode detection gate entirely; the skill starts in what was previously Step 1 of building mode. Remove heavy architecture loading from Step 1 — rely on codebase-investigator and internet-researcher only. Add a new Step 4 Architecture Impact Check that runs after design presentation: 5 structural questions against the designed solution, routing to /intuition if any yes. Add a design-time friction trigger to the design presentation step: when the skill detects awkwardness, shim language, or pattern contradiction, offer /intuition. Restructure the epic template per the agreed section list. Consolidate the rules section to 10 critical rules. After the core SKILL.md rewrite, audit secondary files (commands/brainstorm.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md) for stale references and update as needed. Bump plugin.json version.

## Architecture
Files affected:
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (full rewrite)
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (version bump)
- commands/brainstorm.md (check and update if thinking-mode references exist)
- CLAUDE.md (check and update if thinking-mode or heavy-architecture-loading references exist)
- README.md (check and update if thinking-mode references exist)

Files NOT affected (integration surface preserved):
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md (existing bidirectional integration via Resolution Protocol's "Brainstorm" option is sufficient)
- skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md (Architecture Update Checklist already lives there)
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md (handoff interface unchanged)

## Architecture Impact
(Assessed against the designed solution.)

- [ ] Creates a new component/module — NO (Consider skill is separate epic)
- [x] Changes public interface of existing component — YES (brainstorming skill's behavioral API changes: new Step 4, template restructuring, no thinking mode)
- [ ] Adds/removes cross-component dependencies — NO (integration with intuition, executing-plans, sre-task-refinement all preserved)
- [ ] Creates new request path through 2+ components — NO (workflow path brainstorm -> sre-refinement -> executing-plans unchanged)
- [x] Moves responsibility between components — YES (thinking-mode responsibility moves OUT of brainstorming to a future Consider skill)

Result: Two boxes checked — responsibility movement (thinking mode -> future Consider skill) and behavioral-interface change (skill's API). /intuition NOT invoked here because intuition analyzes codebase architecture (import graphs, co-change, module structure), not skill-framework architecture. The skill-framework redesign analysis happened through extended design conversation documented in this epic's Design Rationale. If the redesign introduces codebase-level tensions later (e.g., future /consider skill + brainstorming skill create confusing invocation paths), /intuition should be run at that point.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The brainstorming skill has grown to ~1000+ lines with 11 critical rules, dual mode paths (thinking/building), heavy upfront architecture loading, and template sprawl (9 top-level sections with 18+ subsections). Conversations driven by Claude 4.7 suggest long skill files, complex branching, and elaborate templates hurt performance: 4.7 is stronger at strict instruction-following but weaker with long context and ambiguity. The skill has accumulated cruft from multiple rewrites without consolidation — Design Discovery and Design Rationale duplicate each other; Architecture Context duplicates what intuition already provides; mode detection complexity exists mainly to serve thinking mode, which does almost no work.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:1023-1036 — current 11 critical rules; rules 1-4 exist only to gate thinking mode
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:378-539 — current epic template, 9 top-level sections, 18+ total subsections
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:400-419 — Architecture Context and Architecture Update Checklist; redundant with intuition and review-implementation respectively
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:503-539 — Design Discovery section; overlaps substantially with Design Rationale
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md — intuition skill already provides cybernetic architectural analysis; has bidirectional integration with brainstorming via Resolution Protocol's 'Brainstorm' option; supports scoped invocation (/intuition <scope>, /intuition bd-N)
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md Step 3 Self-Check — explicitly removes recommendation/severity language (cybernetic principle already codified)

**External:** None because this is a skill-framework refactor; no external libraries or APIs involved.

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | Answer | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| Thinking mode in brainstorming or separate skill? | Separate /consider skill | This epic is building-only |
| MTG blue card name for thinking skill? | Consider | New skill is separate work, out of scope |
| Heavy architecture loading in Step 1? | Cut entirely | Rely on intuition when arch analysis is needed |
| Impact Check before or after design? | After (against designed solution, not upfront vibes) | Step 4 runs after Step 3 (Present Design) |
| Impact Check as gate or record? | Routing mechanism | Offers /intuition, doesn't halt |
| SRE refinement stay mandatory? | Yes (flagged as judgement slip when I proposed softening) | Do not soften; framework strength |
| How to handle 'None' escape hatch in template? | Require grounded justification | Rule 10: "None" requires [specific reason] |
| Mid-build friction escape hatch in brainstorming? | No — belongs in executor (future work) | Out of scope for this epic |
| Full rewrite or targeted edits? | Full rewrite | Cruft IS the structure; targeted edits would leave seams |

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Full rewrite of skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (chosen)

**What it is:** Write a new SKILL.md from the ground up with the agreed structure. No attempt to preserve current file organization.

**Investigation:** Extended multi-turn design conversation; validated section-by-section against intuition, executing-plans, review-implementation; confirmed load-bearing behaviors (immutable requirements, anti-patterns, one-task iteration, SRE refinement) are preserved in the design.

**Pros:**
- Clean slate eliminates cruft
- Consistent structure top to bottom
- No risk of orphaned thinking-mode refs or stale cross-references
- Addresses 4.7 performance concerns (length + branching) head-on

**Cons:**
- More work than targeted edits
- Risk of losing nuance from current file that wasn't explicitly called out during design

**Chosen because:** Current file has accumulated cruft from multiple rewrites; targeted edits would leave seams. The comprehensive design review captures load-bearing behaviors. Full rewrite serves the 4.7-tuning goal directly.

#### 2. Targeted edits to existing file (rejected)

**What it is:** Remove thinking mode, delete specified sections, add Step 4, update rules, but preserve existing file structure elsewhere.

**Why explored:** Lower risk of unintended changes; easier diff review.

**Investigation:** Considered during design; analyzed whether the current structure could be trimmed incrementally.

**Pros:**
- Smaller diff; easier review

**Cons:**
- Preserves cruft and inconsistent section ordering
- Leaves seams between new and old sections
- Doesn't meaningfully reduce overall file length (4.7 performance concern)

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Cruft IS the structure. Targeted edits can't reduce complexity without the full-rewrite discipline. User's 4.7 performance concern is about overall file length and branching, which targeted edits don't fully address.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Design review reveals specific load-bearing content in the current file that the rewrite plan doesn't preserve, AND that content cannot be cleanly added to a rewrite.

#### 3. Minimal changes: add two rules and Impact Check only (rejected)

**What it is:** Keep current skill mostly as-is; add rule 8 (offer /intuition on friction), rule 9 (Impact Check required), and the Architecture Impact template section.

**Why explored:** Smallest possible change; preserves all existing behavior.

**Investigation:** Considered early in design before the user surfaced 4.7 performance concerns and cruft accumulation.

**Pros:**
- Minimal disruption
- No risk of losing current functionality

**Cons:**
- Doesn't address file bloat (the primary concern)
- Doesn't remove intuition redundancy
- Doesn't address template sprawl or rule accretion
- Makes the file worse (additive-only)

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** User's primary concern is file growth and cruft; additive-only changes make that worse, not better. The whole point of this epic is simplification.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** 4.7 performance concerns prove unfounded and the user re-prioritizes stability over simplification.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Full rewrite of skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md per agreed structure
- Version bump in .claude-plugin/plugin.json
- Audit and update of secondary files that reference removed features (commands/brainstorm.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md)
- Verification that intuition and review-implementation do not need corresponding updates

**Out of scope:**
- Creation of /consider skill (tracked as separate follow-up epic)
- Executor agent updates for mid-build friction escape hatch (future work, surfaced during this design but deferred)
- Post-build retrospective skill (user's problem, handling separately)
- Changes to intuition skill (bidirectional integration surface already sufficient)
- Changes to review-implementation skill (Architecture Update Checklist already lives there correctly)

### Open Questions
- Does commands/brainstorm.md reference thinking mode or mode detection? (decide during implementation — check and update if so)
- Does CLAUDE.md describe brainstorming's thinking/building split? (decide during implementation — check and update if so)
- Does README.md reference thinking mode? (decide during implementation — check and update if so)

title: Create architectural-audit skill (SKILL.md + command)
design:
## Goal
Create the architectural-audit skill that examines architecture graphs and codebase for complection using Hickey's simplicity framework, producing structured tension reports without recommendations.

## Effort Estimate
6-10 hours (documentation-heavy: ~400 line SKILL.md + command file)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing skill patterns
Read these files to internalize the structure and conventions:
- skills/volatility-decomposition/SKILL.md — sibling skill (graph producer, Socratic)
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md — skill structure reference
- skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md — ADR format reference

### 2. Create skills/architectural-audit/SKILL.md

Required sections (in order):
1. skill_overview
2. rigidity_level — RIGID (analytical, no adaptation — same 6 passes every time, same output format)
3. quick_reference
4. when_to_use
5. the_process — Steps 0-3 (context loading, evidence gathering, 6 analysis passes, self-check + present)
6. examples — 4 examples
7. critical_rules
8. verification_checklist
9. integration
10. resources

### 3. The Process

**Step 0 — Load Architecture Graph:**
- bd list --label arch --type epic --status open
- If found: load epic + all component nodes
- If not found: suggest /decompose first (cannot audit without graph)
- Read existing ADRs: ls doc/arch/adr-*.md

**Step 1 — Gather Evidence (codebase-investigator):**
- ANALYTICAL: No AskUserQuestion. Gather evidence silently.
- If codebase exists: dispatch codebase-investigator with comprehensive prompt:
  - Module structure and boundaries
  - Import/dependency graphs between modules
  - Co-change patterns (files that change together)
  - Actual call patterns between modules
  - Interface surface area (what's exported vs internal)
- Evidence feeds ALL 6 analysis passes
- If no codebase (greenfield with graph only): skip, analysis uses graph evidence only

**Step 2 — Run 6 Analysis Passes (Hickey Framework):**
Each pass produces tensions in the structured format.

Pass 1: Complection Scan
- Look for concerns braided together
- Evidence: import graphs, component responsibilities spanning multiple concerns
- Graph check: components with multiple volatility axes

Pass 2: Interface Analysis
- Look for interfaces that carry more than one concern
- Evidence: exported functions/types, component interface contracts
- Graph check: edges with multiple interaction types

Pass 3: Temporal Coupling
- Look for things that must happen in order but aren't explicitly sequenced
- Evidence: co-change patterns, initialization order dependencies
- Graph check: missing edges (components that interact but have no declared relationship)

Pass 4: Hidden Dependencies
- Look for dependencies not visible in the graph
- Evidence: actual call patterns vs declared edges, shared state, global configuration
- Graph check: components with no declared dependencies but actual code coupling

Pass 5: Layer Violations
- Look for violations of Lowy layer rules
- Evidence: upward calls (Engine calling Manager), business logic in Resource Accessors
- Graph check: layer assignments vs actual behavior

Pass 6: State/Identity Conflation
- Look for mutable shared state, identity conflation
- Evidence: shared mutable state between components, identity used where value would suffice
- Graph check: relates_to edges that actually represent shared state

**Step 3 — Self-Check + Present:**
Before presenting, scan output for accidental recommendations:
- Keywords: "should", "must", "recommend", "better", "prefer", "consider changing"
- Severity words: "critical", "high", "medium", "low", "severe"
- Phrases: "however I think", "the best approach", "you should"
- If found: rewrite to neutral observation format

Present tension report with structured format.

### 4. Tension Report Format

```
## Tension: [Name]
**Components:** [A], [B]
**Pull 1:** [Structural choice] — Gain: [benefit]. Cost: [tradeoff].
**Pull 2:** [Alternative choice] — Gain: [benefit]. Cost: [tradeoff].
**If you assume:** [condition], then Pull 1 is correct.
**If you assume:** [condition], then Pull 2 is correct.
**Structural observation:** [neutral fact, e.g., "affects 3 downstream nodes"]
**Evidence:** [what codebase/graph evidence surfaced this]
```

### 5. ADR-Aware Behavior
- Read all ADRs before analysis
- If a tension matches an accepted ADR decision: skip it (already accepted)
- If codebase evidence contradicts an ADR: report as DRIFT (not tension)

### 6. Drift Report Format

```
## Drift: [ADR-NNN title]
**Declared:** [What the ADR says]
**Observed:** [What the codebase actually does]
**Evidence:** [Specific files, imports, or patterns]
```

### 7. Create commands/audit-arch.md

Matching commands/brainstorm.md pattern.

## Examples to Write (4 required)

### Example 1: Good audit finding real tensions
- Scenario: Audit of order processing graph finds temporal coupling between pricing and fulfillment
- Shows: proper evidence gathering, structured tension format, no recommendations

### Example 2: BAD — Audit that makes recommendations
- Scenario: Audit output says "You should merge these components" or ranks tensions by severity
- Shows: why_it_fails (denies architect agency, implies solution, severity = recommendation)
- Correction: rewrite with neutral observations and both pulls

### Example 3: Good audit detecting ADR drift
- Scenario: ADR says "payments isolated" but codebase shows direct payment calls from order logic
- Shows: drift detection format, evidence-based reporting

### Example 4: BAD — Audit that skips self-check
- Scenario: Output contains "critical tension" and "should be addressed first"
- Shows: why_it_fails (severity ranking = recommendation, "should" = recommendation)
- Correction: self-check catches keywords, rewrites to neutral format

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/architectural-audit/SKILL.md exists with all 10 sections
- [ ] commands/audit-arch.md exists with frontmatter + single-line invocation
- [ ] Process is ANALYTICAL (no AskUserQuestion during analysis)
- [ ] 6 analysis passes map to Hickey's framework
- [ ] Tension report uses exact structured format (tension name, components, both pulls, conditional assumptions)
- [ ] Self-check step with concrete keyword detection patterns
- [ ] ADR reading to skip accepted tensions
- [ ] Drift detection between ADRs and codebase
- [ ] Codebase-investigator dispatched as standard early step feeding all 6 passes
- [ ] All 4 examples written
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing
- [ ] Skill tested with structural verification

## Anti-Patterns
- NO recommendations in output (both pulls, no preference)
- NO severity rankings (structural observations only)
- NO AskUserQuestion during analysis (analytical, not Socratic)
- NO placeholder text
- NO skipping examples

Preserve load-bearing parts: bd epic 7-section template, anti-patterns enforcement, /intuition routing offers, SRE refinement step. Target: ~400-450 lines.

title: Task 3: Move Model Bootstrapping section from /intuition Step 4 to /ponder BOOTSTRAP (simplify dispatch, drop input template)
description: Simplify /intuition Step 4 Model Bootstrapping subsection: remove inline BOOTSTRAP input template; replace with brief dispatch pointer to /ponder BOOTSTRAP. Preserve guard conditions ('Only bootstrap when'). /ponder BOOTSTRAP mode already constructs input from caller context.
design:
## Goal
Remove the inline BOOTSTRAP input template from skills/intuition/SKILL.md Step 4 Model Bootstrapping subsection (lines ~704-731). Replace with a brief dispatch pointer that defers input construction to /ponder BOOTSTRAP mode (already documented in skills/ponder/SKILL.md:51-52, lines 95-104). Preserve the guard conditions ("Only bootstrap when: (a) no docs/arch/*.c4 files exist, AND (b) Resolve decision creates a meaningful boundary"). Update /intuition integration section references to match.

After this task: /intuition Step 4 Model Bootstrapping is ~10 lines (was ~28); /intuition does not template BOOTSTRAP input; the guard conditions are preserved verbatim; /ponder BOOTSTRAP mode is the single owner of input construction.

## This task does NOT touch
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (Task 4)
- agents/ponder.md (already has BOOTSTRAP procedure; no changes needed)
- CLAUDE.md / README.md (final cross-reference task)
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (final-push task)
- Pass 7-9 numbering (already done in bd-bkv)
- Step 0 / Prose Focus / freshness check / Mode references (already fixed in bd-428)
- Anti-recommendation rules 1-4 (preserved verbatim)
- Resolution Protocol Step 4 5 options (Accept / Resolve / Brainstorm / Investigate / Skip)
- Architect Questions
- Step 2 / 9 passes (no changes)

## This task DOES touch
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md Step 4 Model Bootstrapping subsection (lines ~704-731)
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md line ~669 (trigger reference inside Resolve subsection — verify wording still consistent)
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md integration section (lines ~1327, 1346, 1354, 1375 — verify ponder bootstrap references stay accurate)
- POSSIBLY skills/ponder/SKILL.md if BOOTSTRAP input description needs strengthening to absorb /intuition's prior templating semantics. Read /ponder/SKILL.md:51-104 first; only edit if input description is unclear about how the caller provides component info.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (focused, smaller than prior tasks). Mostly section simplification + verification.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN in this task)
- ❌ NO losing the "Only bootstrap when:" guard condition (premature bootstrapping is the failure mode the guard prevents)
- ❌ NO replacing the entire Model Bootstrapping subsection with a one-liner that loses the guard semantics
- ❌ NO removing the cross-reference to /ponder BOOTSTRAP entirely (the architect needs to know that /ponder owns this)
- ❌ NO modifying anti-recommendation rules 1-4
- ❌ NO touching Resolution Protocol's 5 options
- ❌ NO touching Pass numbering (already 1-9 from bd-bkv)
- ❌ NO touching Step 0 / Prose Focus (already fixed in bd-428)
- ❌ NO regressing /intuition line count below 1410 (this task should reduce, not grow)
- ❌ NO partial commits (commit only when verification passes)
- ❌ NO modifying agents/ponder.md (BOOTSTRAP procedure is already complete there)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md:660-731 (Step 4 Resolve + Model Bootstrapping)
- Read skills/intuition/SKILL.md:1320-1380 (integration section — ponder references)
- Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md:41-104 (Mode Detection + Dispatch Protocol — confirm BOOTSTRAP input construction)
- Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md:230-260 (BOOTSTRAP example — confirm structured input format)
- Read agents/ponder.md BOOTSTRAP section (verify procedure is complete; no edits needed)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD — RED phase)

Save as /tmp/verify-bd-task3.sh, chmod +x, run pre-edit (must FAIL) then post-edit (must PASS):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
F=skills/intuition/SKILL.md
P=skills/ponder/SKILL.md

echo "=== /intuition: BOOTSTRAP input template removed ==="
! grep -F 'Mode: BOOTSTRAP' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: 'Mode: BOOTSTRAP' template marker still in /intuition"; exit 1; }
! grep -F 'System name: [from codebase or architect input]' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: BOOTSTRAP input template skeleton still in /intuition"; exit 1; }
! grep -E 'Components:.*\[componentA\]' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: BOOTSTRAP placeholder components still in /intuition"; exit 1; }
! grep -F 'Source: Intuition audit without architecture model' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: BOOTSTRAP source line still in /intuition (template fragment)"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /intuition: guard conditions preserved ==="
grep -F 'Only bootstrap when' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: 'Only bootstrap when' guard missing"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'no docs/arch/*.c4 files exist' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: guard condition (a) missing"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'Resolve decision creates a meaningful boundary|first Resolve decision' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: guard condition (b) missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /intuition: /ponder cross-reference present ==="
grep -E '/ponder|hyperpowers:ponder' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: /ponder cross-reference missing"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'BOOTSTRAP mode' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: BOOTSTRAP mode reference missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /ponder: BOOTSTRAP input construction documented ==="
grep -E 'Mode: BOOTSTRAP' "$P" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: /ponder BOOTSTRAP mode marker missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== Anti-recommendation rules preserved verbatim ==="
grep -F 'NO recommendations' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 1 missing"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'NO severity rankings' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 2 missing"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'NO ordering or prioritization' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 3 missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /intuition: 9 passes preserved (regression check) ==="
grep -E 'Run ALL 9 passes' "$F" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: 'Run ALL 9 passes' rule missing"; exit 1; }
! grep -E '^### Pass 10' "$F" || { echo "FAIL: Pass 10 reappeared"; exit 1; }

echo "=== Line count: at least 12 more lines reduced from /intuition ==="
LINES=$(wc -l < "$F")
[ "$LINES" -le 1398 ] || { echo "FAIL: /intuition line count $LINES > 1398 (started 1410; expected -12 from bootstrap simplification)"; exit 1; }

echo "All checks passed. /intuition: $LINES lines."
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Section A: Verify /ponder already owns BOOTSTRAP input construction**

- [ ] Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md:41-104 carefully. Confirm BOOTSTRAP mode dispatch protocol describes how the caller provides component info. The current protocol says "Components: [list with descriptions and layers]; Known relationships: [if any]". This is sufficient — the caller (in this case /intuition Step 4 Resolve) provides the component info naturally from the tension being resolved. NO edit to /ponder/SKILL.md required.
- [ ] If /ponder/SKILL.md BOOTSTRAP description is unclear or thin, ADD a brief paragraph: "For BOOTSTRAP dispatched from /intuition Step 4 Resolve, the caller provides component info from the tension being resolved (component names, descriptions, relationships). /ponder constructs the structured input." This is a fallback only if the existing description is insufficient.

**Section B: Simplify /intuition Step 4 Model Bootstrapping subsection**

- [ ] skills/intuition/SKILL.md:704-731 — Replace the section with a simplified version. Target structure:
  ```
  ### Model Bootstrapping (via /ponder)

  When a "Resolve" decision creates the first architectural boundary and no architecture model exists, dispatch /ponder in BOOTSTRAP mode. /ponder constructs the structured input from the resolved tension's evidence; do not template the input here.

  **Only bootstrap when:** (a) no docs/arch/*.c4 files exist, AND (b) a Resolve decision creates a meaningful boundary between modules. Do not bootstrap for Accept or Skip decisions.

  **Dispatch:** Invoke /ponder with the resolved tension's context (component names, descriptions, the relationship just decided). /ponder BOOTSTRAP mode handles all file creation (spec.c4, model.c4, component .c4 files, views, markdown docs), quality criteria enforcement, and validation. See skills/ponder/SKILL.md BOOTSTRAP mode for input construction details.

  After /ponder returns, announce: "Architecture model bootstrapped via /ponder. Future audits will load the model."
  ```
- [ ] Verify the simplified section is ~10 lines (down from ~28).

**Section C: Verify Resolve trigger line still consistent**

- [ ] skills/intuition/SKILL.md:669 — current text: "If this is the first boundary being created and no model exists: trigger **Model Bootstrapping** (see below)" — should remain unchanged. Confirm cross-reference still works (Model Bootstrapping subsection still exists, just shorter).

**Section D: Verify integration section references**

- [ ] skills/intuition/SKILL.md:1327, 1346, 1354, 1375 (approximate; line numbers may shift after edit) — find any references to "Step 4 bootstrap", "/ponder bootstrap", etc. Verify wording still accurate after the simplification. No need to expand them; just confirm they're not contradicted.

**Section E: Pre-commit + verify**

- [ ] Run `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task3.sh` against pre-edit file; confirm it FAILS (RED phase).
- [ ] Make edits per Sections A-D.
- [ ] Run `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task3.sh` against post-edit file; confirm "All checks passed."
- [ ] Manual spot-check: read the simplified Model Bootstrapping subsection; verify guard conditions present, /ponder cross-reference present, no input template fragments remain.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass.
- [ ] Commit (single commit; do not amend).

## Success Criteria

**Verification grep checks (must all pass):**
- [ ] `! grep -F 'Mode: BOOTSTRAP' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (no template marker in /intuition)
- [ ] `! grep -F 'System name: [from codebase or architect input]' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (no template skeleton)
- [ ] `! grep -E 'Components:.*\[componentA\]' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (no placeholder components)
- [ ] `! grep -F 'Source: Intuition audit without architecture model' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (no source line fragment)
- [ ] `grep -F 'Only bootstrap when' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (guard preserved)
- [ ] `grep -F 'no docs/arch/*.c4 files exist' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (guard condition (a))
- [ ] `grep -E '/ponder|hyperpowers:ponder' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (cross-reference present)
- [ ] `grep -E 'BOOTSTRAP mode' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (mode reference present)

**Content preservation checks (must all pass):**
- [ ] `grep -F 'NO recommendations' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (rule 1 verbatim)
- [ ] `grep -F 'NO severity rankings' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (rule 2 verbatim)
- [ ] `grep -F 'NO ordering or prioritization' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (rule 3 verbatim)
- [ ] `grep -E 'Run ALL 9 passes' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (rule 5 preserved post-bd-bkv)
- [ ] `! grep -E '^### Pass 10' skills/intuition/SKILL.md` succeeds (no Pass 10 regression)

**Quantitative check:**
- [ ] `wc -l skills/intuition/SKILL.md` ≤ 1398 (started 1410; ≥12-line reduction)

**Manual checks:**
- [ ] Simplified Model Bootstrapping section reads coherently (one paragraph + guard + dispatch + announcement)
- [ ] Guard conditions clearly visible
- [ ] /ponder cross-reference visible
- [ ] No input-template fragments remain in /intuition
- [ ] Resolve subsection trigger line (~669) still cross-references correctly
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Edge Cases & Failure Modes

**/ponder BOOTSTRAP description insufficient for caller info:**
- The /ponder dispatch protocol expects a structured input ("Mode: BOOTSTRAP\n\nSystem name: ...\nComponents: ...\n..."). After this task, /intuition Step 4 dispatches /ponder with prose context describing the resolved tension; /ponder may need its dispatch protocol clarified to handle this case.
- Mitigation: read /ponder/SKILL.md BOOTSTRAP example carefully. If the example shows /ponder constructing structured input from caller knowledge, no /ponder edit needed. If /ponder requires the caller to pre-template, then either /intuition must keep some templating (against the trim goal) or /ponder must be updated.
- Conservative path: if /ponder is unclear, add a one-paragraph note to skills/ponder/SKILL.md describing how a caller (e.g., /intuition Step 4) provides BOOTSTRAP context as prose; /ponder constructs the structured input internally. This is small and removes ambiguity.

**Guard condition phrasing changes during simplification:**
- The current guard says: "Only bootstrap when: (a) no docs/arch/*.c4 files exist, AND (b) a Resolve decision creates a meaningful boundary between modules."
- Verify exactly this phrasing (or semantically equivalent) is preserved after edit. Do NOT shorten "meaningful boundary between modules" to "boundary" — the qualifier matters.

**Resolve subsection cross-reference breaks:**
- Line 669 in current file says "trigger **Model Bootstrapping** (see below)". After simplification, the "below" section still exists (~10 lines instead of ~28); cross-reference remains accurate. Verify the **Model Bootstrapping** header is preserved as a section heading so the markdown anchor still resolves.

**Integration section references stale:**
- Lines ~1327, 1346, 1354, 1375 reference /ponder bootstrap. They should remain accurate after simplification (we're keeping the dispatch path, just removing the input templating). Verify by reading.

**Concurrent edits:**
- Single-author refactor; no concurrency concerns.

## Notes for next task
After this task completes, the next iteration will:
- Task 4: Update skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md friction-detection prompt and AIC-routing prompt to use prose focus instead of --scope flag. (Brainstorm currently says "/intuition can systematically examine your architecture" without prose-focus mechanism; should pass focus context naturally.)
- Task 5 (final): CLAUDE.md + README.md cross-references update + .claude-plugin/plugin.json minor version bump (e.g., 3.7.0 -> 3.8.0).

title: Rewrite executing-plans skill to use native Claude Code features
description: The executing-plans skill (~870 lines) reimplements agent orchestration that Claude Code now provides natively. As Claude Code evolves, these custom implementations increasingly conflict with built-in behavior, causing three failure modes: non-activation, drift to native behavior, and executor lifecycle breaks. This epic rewrites the skill to a ~200-300 line declarative validation protocol that leans into native features instead of fighting them.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
1. The executing-plans skill must be 300 lines or fewer (down from ~870)
2. The skill must NOT implement custom health monitoring (no timer logic)
3. The skill must NOT implement custom shutdown protocol (no shutdown_request/response handshake)
4. The skill must NOT implement naming collision recovery
5. Executors must use native TaskCreate/TaskUpdate for TDD sub-step tracking
6. SendMessage is used ONLY for: completion reports, escalations, and lead corrections
7. Lead must validate every proposal against epic requirements/anti-patterns/Design Discovery (core value preserved)
8. Project memory bridge must be preserved (executor writes learnings before completing, next executor reads on startup)
9. Reviewer dispatch as subagent must be preserved
10. Per-task executor with fresh context must be preserved (spawn fresh executor per task)
11. Old executor idles out naturally via Claude Code native timeout — no explicit shutdown needed
12. bd remains the strategic task tracker (epics and tasks in bd, sub-steps in native tasks)
13. The executor agent prompt (executor.md) must be aligned with the new skill
14. All files referencing executing-plans must be updated for consistency
15. The skill must describe WHAT the lead validates, not HOW to manage agents

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] executing-plans SKILL.md is 300 lines or fewer
- [ ] executor.md agent aligned with new skill (no shutdown protocol, no health timer references, uses TaskCreate for sub-steps)
- [ ] reviewer.md has no stale references to old orchestration model
- [ ] commands/execute-plan.md description aligned with new pattern
- [ ] Cross-referencing skills updated: brainstorming, review-implementation, finishing-branch, using-hyper, writing-plans, dispatching-parallel-agents, managing-bd-tasks, analyzing-test-effectiveness
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated to reflect new architecture (Agent System section, Core Workflows section)
- [ ] README.md updated if it describes the old orchestration model
- [ ] Zero references to: custom health monitoring, shutdown_request protocol, naming collision recovery, 10-min/5-min timers in any skill/agent/command file
- [ ] Skill describes validation protocol (what to check) not orchestration mechanics (how to manage agents)
- [ ] Native TaskCreate/TaskUpdate documented as executor progress tracking mechanism
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO custom health monitoring in any form (reason: conflicts with native 5-min heartbeat, was the primary source of orchestration complexity and drift)
- ❌ NO shutdown_request/shutdown_response protocol (reason: executor idles out naturally via native timeout, explicit shutdown caused lifecycle breaks and name collisions)
- ❌ NO naming collision recovery code (reason: accept whatever name Claude Code assigns, track it — no workaround needed)
- ❌ NO TeamCreate/TeamDelete instructions in the skill (reason: Claude Code manages team lifecycle — the skill should not instruct how to create/delete teams)
- ❌ NO timer logic of any kind (reason: 10-min + 5-min timers conflicted with native heartbeat and caused false recoveries)
- ❌ NO custom SendMessage types for progress tracking (reason: use native TaskCreate/TaskUpdate instead — SendMessage docs say 'Don't send structured JSON status messages — use TaskUpdate')
- ❌ NO reimplementing agent lifecycle management (reason: the whole point is leaning into native features)
- ❌ NO expanding scope beyond executing-plans ecosystem (reason: only touch files that reference executing-plans directly)
- ❌ NO adding new files (reason: this is a rewrite of existing files, not new feature development)

## Approach
Rewrite the executing-plans skill from an 870-line orchestration state machine to a ~200-300 line declarative validation protocol. The skill shifts from describing HOW to manage agents (timers, health checks, shutdown protocols, naming recovery) to describing WHAT the lead validates (epic compliance, anti-patterns, Design Discovery). Agent lifecycle is delegated entirely to Claude Code's native features. Executor progress tracking uses native TaskCreate/TaskUpdate instead of custom SendMessage protocol. Old executors idle out naturally instead of explicit shutdown. The executor agent prompt is aligned to match. All cross-referencing files are updated for consistency.

## Architecture
Files to modify:
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — full rewrite (core deliverable)
- agents/executor.md — rewrite for new lifecycle model
- agents/reviewer.md — update stale references
- commands/execute-plan.md — simplify description
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md — update handoff section
- skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md — update executor references
- skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md — update team cleanup references
- skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md — update if references old model
- CLAUDE.md — update Agent System and Core Workflows sections
- README.md — update orchestration description

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The executing-plans skill (~870 lines) reimplements agent orchestration that Claude Code now provides natively — health monitoring, shutdown protocols, naming collision recovery, message routing. As Claude Code evolves, these custom implementations increasingly conflict with built-in behavior, causing three failure modes: skill doesn't activate at all (too many conflicting instructions), partial activation with drift (starts following skill then falls back to native behavior), and executor lifecycle breaks (name collisions, failed shutdowns, orphaned executors).

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — 870 lines, heavily procedural, implements custom state machine for agent lifecycle
- agents/executor.md — 386 lines, includes shutdown_request/response handling, health timer references, SendMessage progress protocol
- agents/reviewer.md — clean subagent pattern, minimal changes needed
- Memory files document prior fixes: name collision on respawn (commit 3f5c5b7), executor forgetting to commit

**External:**
- Claude Code TeamCreate docs confirm: teammates CAN use TaskCreate/TaskList/TaskUpdate — verified by live test
- Claude Code SendMessage docs explicitly say: 'Don't send structured JSON status messages — use TaskUpdate'
- Claude Code native agent teams have 5-min heartbeat timeout, auto-inactive on crash
- Teammates go idle after turns — this is normal, not an error state
- Teams share task lists at ~/.claude/tasks/{team-name}/

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Declarative Validation Protocol ✓

**What it is:** Strip skill to ~200-300 lines focused on WHAT the lead validates (epic compliance, anti-patterns, Design Discovery) rather than HOW to manage agents. Delegate all orchestration to Claude Code's native features. Use TaskCreate/TaskUpdate for executor progress tracking.

**Investigation:**
- Verified teammates can use TaskCreate (live test — confirmed)
- Verified SendMessage docs recommend TaskUpdate over structured messages
- Analyzed which parts of current skill are validation vs orchestration — ~300 lines validation, ~550 lines orchestration

**Pros:**
- Eliminates all three failure modes (non-activation, drift, lifecycle breaks)
- Less instruction surface = less conflict with native behavior
- Future-proof as Claude Code evolves
- Aligns with how Claude Code is designed to work

**Cons:**
- Less explicit control over executor behavior
- Relies on Claude Code's orchestration being good enough

**Chosen because:** Root cause of all failures is fighting native behavior. Leaning in eliminates the conflict entirely.

#### 2. Thin Orchestration Wrapper ❌

**What it is:** Keep minimal orchestration but align with native features (~400-500 lines). Remove health monitoring but keep SendMessage coordination and explicit shutdown.

**Why we looked at this:** Seemed like a middle ground — less risky than full rewrite.

**Investigation:**
- Estimated ~400-500 lines still needed
- Would still partially reimplement harness behavior
- Would still have moderate conflict risk with native features

**Pros:** More explicit control, familiar pattern

**Cons:** Still partially reimplements harness behavior, moderate conflict risk remains, more instructions = more drift surface

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Still competes with native behavior on orchestration. Moderate conflict risk means the same problems recur as Claude Code evolves.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Claude Code removes native agent lifecycle management entirely.

#### 3. Subagent Pattern (No Teams) ❌

**What it is:** Replace teammates with one-shot subagents (Agent tool without team_name). No naming, no teams, no SendMessage — just input→output.

**Why we looked at this:** Simplest possible model, eliminates all lifecycle issues.

**Investigation:**
- Would lose real-time progress visibility entirely
- Lead waits blindly for completion — no checkpoint messages
- Longer feedback loops for escalations

**Pros:** Eliminates all naming/lifecycle/messaging issues entirely

**Cons:** Loses real-time progress visibility, lead waits blindly, longer feedback loops

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Loses escalation ability and progress visibility, which are core value propositions.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Claude Code adds a way for subagents to send progress updates back to the caller.

#### 4. Just Fix Friction Points ❌

**What it is:** Keep current 870-line architecture but patch specific conflicts: naming collisions, health check timing, auto-plan-mode interference.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal change, targeted fixes.

**Investigation:**
- Already did this once (commit 3f5c5b7 for naming collision)
- Problems keep recurring as Claude Code evolves
- Each patch adds complexity to an already complex skill

**Pros:** Minimal change

**Cons:** Band-aid approach, problems recur with each Claude Code update, skill grows more complex over time

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Root cause is architectural conflict, not individual bugs. Patches don't address the fundamental tension.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The rewrite proves too disruptive and we need a stop-gap.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Rewriting executing-plans SKILL.md
- Rewriting executor.md agent
- Updating reviewer.md for stale references
- Updating all cross-referencing files (skills, commands, CLAUDE.md, README.md)

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Rewriting other skills (brainstorming, review-implementation, etc.) beyond updating their executing-plans references
- Adding new skills or agents
- Changing bd integration model
- Changing how brainstorming creates epics
- Changing reviewer logic (it's already a clean subagent pattern)

### Open Questions
- Should the executor still propose the next task, or should the lead determine next tasks independently? (Keep current: executor proposes, lead validates)
- Should the skill mention team creation at all, or let Claude Code handle it implicitly? (Decide during implementation)
- How much of the executor escalation protocol should survive? (Keep it — it's validation logic, not orchestration)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Primary goal? | Lean into native features | Rewrite to use native TaskCreate, team heartbeat, agent lifecycle — integrate with harness |
| Which failures? | Non-activation, drift, lifecycle breaks | All three stem from conflicting with native behavior — root cause is architectural |
| What to keep? | All four: validation, fresh context, memory bridge, reviewer gate | Core value survives; only orchestration mechanics are removed |
| bd integration? | bd strategic + native tasks tactical | bd for epics/tasks (cross-session), TaskCreate for sub-steps (within-session) |
| Executor lifecycle? | Idle out naturally | No explicit shutdown protocol — Claude Code handles it |
| Progress tracking? | TaskCreate (tested, confirmed working) | Executor creates native tasks for TDD sub-steps, lead monitors via TaskList |
| Scope? | Full audit of all related files | Skill + executor + reviewer + commands + cross-referencing skills + CLAUDE.md + README.md |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Teammate Tool Access
**Question explored:** Can teammates use TaskCreate/TaskList/TaskUpdate?
**Sources consulted:**
- Claude Code TeamCreate docs — says teammates 'should create new tasks with TaskCreate'
- Internet researcher community sources — suggested teammates might NOT have TaskCreate access
- Live test — spawned teammate in test-task-tools team, confirmed TaskCreate and TaskList both work

**Findings:**
- TeamCreate docs explicitly mention teammates using TaskCreate
- Live test confirmed: teammate successfully created task #1 and listed it via TaskList
- The internet researcher's community sources were incorrect/outdated

**Conclusion:** Plan A (executor creates own sub-tasks) is confirmed viable

#### Claude Code Native Agent Lifecycle
**Question explored:** How does Claude Code manage agent lifecycle natively?
**Sources consulted:**
- TeamCreate docs — describes idle state, auto-notifications, 5-min heartbeat
- SendMessage docs — 'Don't send structured JSON status messages — use TaskUpdate'
- Agent tool docs — team_name for teammates, no team_name for subagents

**Findings:**
- Teammates go idle after every turn — this is normal, not an error
- Idle teammates can receive messages (sending a message wakes them)
- System auto-sends idle notifications to lead
- No explicit shutdown needed for normal lifecycle

**Conclusion:** Native lifecycle management handles everything the custom health monitoring tried to do

### Dead-End Paths

#### Custom Health Monitoring Refinement
**Why explored:** Could we fix the 10+5 min timer instead of removing it?
**Investigation:** Already patched once (naming collision fix). Timer conflicts fundamentally with native 5-min heartbeat. Each Claude Code update changes timing behavior.
**Why abandoned:** Architectural conflict — no timer value eliminates the tension

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'What if executor dies silently and no one notices?' → Native heartbeat handles this. Idle notification shows executor state. TaskList shows progress stalled.
- 'What if naming collision still happens?' → Accept it. Track whatever name Claude Code assigns. No recovery code needed.
- 'Will reduced instruction surface actually improve activation?' → Strong hypothesis: fewer conflicting instructions = less drift. First real-world use will validate.
close_reason: All 11 success criteria met. Reviewer APPROVED. executing-plans rewritten from 870-line orchestration to 287-line validation protocol. Version bumped to 3.4.0.

title: Phase 5: Stop Hook (Gentle Reminders)
design:
## Goal
Implement hook that shows self-check reminders after Claude responds.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Stop hook created and configured in hooks.json
- [ ] Reads context from edit-log.txt successfully
- [ ] Shows TDD reminder if .ts/.js/.py files edited without test files
- [ ] Shows verification reminder if user says "done" or "complete"
- [ ] Shows commit reminder if >3 files edited in session
- [ ] Non-intrusive output (max 5 lines)
- [ ] Handles missing/empty context gracefully
- [ ] Performance <50ms

## Implementation Steps (ADDED BY writing-plans, REFINED BY SRE)

### Step 1: Create Stop hook directory
Run:
\`\`\`bash
mkdir -p hooks/stop
\`\`\`

### Step 2: Create gentle reminders hook

Create \`hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh\`:

\`\`\`bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
CONTEXT_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/../context"
UTILS_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/../utils"
LOG_FILE="$CONTEXT_DIR/edit-log.txt"
SESSION_START=$(date -d "1 hour ago" +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1H +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# Source utilities (if they exist)
if [ -f "$UTILS_DIR/context-query.sh" ]; then
    source "$UTILS_DIR/context-query.sh"
else
    # Fallback if utilities missing
    get_session_files() {
        if [ -f "$LOG_FILE" ]; then
            awk -F '|' -v since="$SESSION_START" '$1 >= since {gsub(/^[ \\t]+|[ \\t]+$/, "", $4); print $4}' "$LOG_FILE" | sort -u
        fi
    }
fi

# Read response from stdin to check for completion claims
RESPONSE=""
if read -t 1 -r response_json 2>/dev/null; then
    RESPONSE=$(echo "$response_json" | jq -r '.text // ""' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
fi

# Get edited files in this session
EDITED_FILES=$(get_session_files "$SESSION_START" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
FILE_COUNT=$(echo "$EDITED_FILES" | grep -c . 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

# Check patterns for appropriate reminders
SHOW_TDD_REMINDER=false
SHOW_VERIFY_REMINDER=false
SHOW_COMMIT_REMINDER=false

# Check 1: Files edited but no test files?
if [ "$FILE_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    # Check if source files edited
    if echo "$EDITED_FILES" | grep -qE '\\.(ts|js|py|go|rs|java)$' 2>/dev/null; then
        # Check if NO test files edited
        if ! echo "$EDITED_FILES" | grep -qE '(test|spec)\\.(ts|js|py|go|rs|java)$' 2>/dev/null; then
            SHOW_TDD_REMINDER=true
        fi
    fi
    
    # Check 2: Many files edited?
    if [ "$FILE_COUNT" -ge 3 ]; then
        SHOW_COMMIT_REMINDER=true
    fi
fi

# Check 3: User claiming completion?
if echo "$RESPONSE" | grep -iE '(done|complete|finished|ready|works)' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    SHOW_VERIFY_REMINDER=true
fi

# Display appropriate reminders (max 5 lines)
if [ "$SHOW_TDD_REMINDER" = true ] || [ "$SHOW_VERIFY_REMINDER" = true ] || [ "$SHOW_COMMIT_REMINDER" = true ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    
    if [ "$SHOW_TDD_REMINDER" = true ]; then
        echo "💭 Remember: Write tests first (TDD)"
    fi
    
    if [ "$SHOW_VERIFY_REMINDER" = true ]; then
        echo "✅ Before claiming complete: Run tests"
    fi
    
    if [ "$SHOW_COMMIT_REMINDER" = true ]; then
        echo "💾 Consider: $FILE_COUNT files edited - commit?"
    fi
    
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
fi

# Always return success (non-blocking)
exit 0
\`\`\`

### Step 3: Make executable
Run:
\`\`\`bash
chmod +x hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh
\`\`\`

### Step 4: Update hooks.json

Add Stop hook configuration to \`hooks/hooks.json\` (preserve existing entries):

\`\`\`json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [...],
    "PreToolUse": [...],
    "UserPromptSubmit": [...],
    "PostToolUse": [...],
    "Stop": [
      {
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
\`\`\`

### Step 5: Create test script

Create \`hooks/stop/test-reminders.sh\`:

\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Testing Stop Hook Reminders ==="

# Test 1: No edits = no reminder
echo "Test 1: No edits"
> hooks/context/edit-log.txt
echo '{"text": "All done!"}' | bash hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh > /tmp/out1.txt
[ ! -s /tmp/out1.txt ] && echo "✓ No reminder (correct)" || echo "✗ Unexpected reminder"

# Test 2: Source file edited without test = TDD reminder
echo "Test 2: TDD reminder"
echo "$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | hyper | Edit | src/main.ts" > hooks/context/edit-log.txt
echo '{"text": "Feature implemented"}' | bash hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh > /tmp/out2.txt
grep -q "TDD" /tmp/out2.txt && echo "✓ TDD reminder shown" || echo "✗ TDD reminder missing"

# Test 3: Completion claim = verification reminder
echo "Test 3: Verification reminder"
echo '{"text": "All done and tests pass!"}' | bash hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh > /tmp/out3.txt
grep -q "Run tests" /tmp/out3.txt && echo "✓ Verify reminder shown" || echo "✗ Verify reminder missing"

# Test 4: Many files = commit reminder
echo "Test 4: Commit reminder"
for i in {1..5}; do
    echo "$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | hyper | Edit | src/file$i.ts" >> hooks/context/edit-log.txt
done
echo '{"text": "Refactoring complete"}' | bash hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh > /tmp/out4.txt
grep -q "commit" /tmp/out4.txt && echo "✓ Commit reminder shown" || echo "✗ Commit reminder missing"

echo "=== All Tests Complete ==="
\`\`\`

### Step 6: Run tests
Run:
\`\`\`bash
chmod +x hooks/stop/test-reminders.sh
bash hooks/stop/test-reminders.sh
\`\`\`

Expected: All tests pass

### Step 7: Test performance
Run:
\`\`\`bash
time echo '{"text": "Done"}' | bash hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh
\`\`\`

Expected: <50ms execution time

### Step 8: Commit
Run:
\`\`\`bash
git add hooks/stop/10-gentle-reminders.sh hooks/stop/test-reminders.sh hooks/hooks.json
git commit -m "feat(bd-6): implement Stop hook for gentle reminders

Implements bd-6: Stop Hook (Gentle Reminders)
- Shows TDD reminder when source edited without tests
- Shows verification reminder when user claims complete
- Shows commit reminder when many files edited
- Non-intrusive (max 5 lines output)
- Performance <50ms"
\`\`\`

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Context Availability**:
- edit-log.txt may not exist (fresh install)
- PostToolUse hook (bd-5) may not be running
- Must handle gracefully with fallbacks

**Pattern Matching Accuracy**:
- Avoid false positives (e.g., "done" in middle of sentence)
- Source vs test file detection must be accurate
- Consider different naming conventions (test.js, spec.js, _test.go)

**User Experience**:
- Keep output brief (max 5 lines)
- Only show relevant reminders, not all
- Use clear, actionable language

**Performance**:
- Stop hook runs after every Claude response
- Must be fast (<50ms) to not delay user
- Avoid complex processing or external commands

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Annoying/repetitive reminders every response
- ❌ Blocking on I/O or missing files
- ❌ Long multi-paragraph reminders
- ❌ False positive pattern matching
- ❌ Slow execution delaying response

title: Task 2: Update skills to remove pre-commit references
design:
## Goal
Update all skills that reference pre-commit hooks to use the new universal validation terminology ('Run: validate', linter/typecheck/tests).

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Files to Update
Based on earlier grep, these skills reference pre-commit:
1. skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md
2. skills/review-implementation/SKILL.md
3. skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md
4. skills/fixing-bugs/SKILL.md
5. skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md
6. skills/common-patterns/bd-commands.md

## Implementation

### For each file:
1. Search for 'pre-commit' references
2. Replace with appropriate universal terminology:
   - 'Pre-commit hooks pass' → 'Validations pass (lint, typecheck, tests)'
   - 'Run pre-commit' → 'Run: validate'
   - 'pre-commit hooks' → 'project validations'
3. Verify no pre-commit strings remain

### Specific changes:
**verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:**
- Line ~28: Update 'Use test-runner agent for' description
- Line ~146: Update checklist item 'Pre-commit hooks pass'
- Line ~161: Update 'Pre-commit hooks not checked'
- Line ~178: Update comment about pre-commit
- Lines ~295-301: Update/remove 'Pre-Commit Hook Assumption' section

**review-implementation/SKILL.md:**
- Line ~214: Change 'Run: .git/hooks/pre-commit' to 'Run: validate'
- Lines ~455, 476, 508, 543, 560, 682, 690, 700, 728: Update pre-commit references

**finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md:**
- Update Step 2 to use 'Run: validate' instead of pre-commit

**fixing-bugs/SKILL.md:**
- Lines ~180, 501: Update 'Pre-commit hooks pass' to 'Validations pass'

**brainstorming/SKILL.md:**
- Lines ~199, 370: Update success criteria template

**common-patterns/bd-commands.md:**
- Line ~122: Update comment about pre-commit

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `grep -r 'pre-commit' skills/` returns only expected files (debugging references, not test-runner calls)
- [ ] verification-before-completion references 'Run: validate'
- [ ] review-implementation references 'Run: validate'
- [ ] finishing-a-development-branch Step 2 uses 'Run: validate'
- [ ] All success criteria templates updated to 'Validations pass'
- [ ] Changes committed

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO leaving 'pre-commit hooks pass' in checklists (inconsistent)
- ❌ NO references to .git/hooks/pre-commit (doesn't exist in most projects)
- ❌ NO breaking skill functionality (preserve verification intent)
close_reason: Updated all skill files to use universal validation terminology

title: Rewrite skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md with simplified structure
design:
## Goal
Rewrite skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md with the simplified structure from the parent epic. Single-file rewrite.

## Effort Estimate
6-10 hours. Borderline subtask-limit; kept as single task because splitting a single-file rewrite would create seams between partial sections.

## Implementation

### 1. Study current SKILL.md structure to identify content to preserve or cut

Open skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md. Apply explicit preserve/cut lists (do not rely on subjective "load-bearing" judgement):

**PRESERVE (copy or adapt into new file):**
- Critical rules 5-11 from the current `<critical_rules>` section → become new rules 1-7 (renumbered)
- All three examples from the current `<examples>` section (the ones showing: skipping research, full task tree upfront, epic without anti-patterns)
- `<key_principles>` section — core principles list
- `<research_agents>` section — codebase-investigator vs internet-researcher guidance
- `<integration>` section structure (update references but preserve shape)
- `<resources>` section

**CUT (do not copy):**
- Step 0 Mode Detection Gate (entire section)
- Thinking mode path content (all text under "THINKING MODE path" and related)
- Heavy architecture loading in Step 1 (LikeC4 MCP calls, ADR scanning, dynamic view loading, Mode 1/Mode 2 machinery — all of it)
- Epic template section: Architecture Context
- Epic template section: Architecture Update Checklist
- Epic template section: Design Discovery (entire)
- Critical rules 1-4 (all mode-detection rules)
- `<common_excuses>` items referencing thinking mode or mode detection

### 2. Study intuition skill integration surface

Open skills/intuition/SKILL.md. Focus on Step 4 Resolution Protocol. Understand bidirectional handoff:
- brainstorm → intuition: on design-time friction (new Step 3 trigger); on Impact Check result (new Step 4)
- intuition → brainstorm: Step 4 Resolution Protocol "Brainstorm" option hands off with tension as context

Integration section in new SKILL.md must document BOTH directions.

### 3. Write new skill file section by section
File path: skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md

Sections in order:
- Frontmatter: name: brainstorming, description updated to reflect building-only scope (single-sentence description)
- skill_overview: "Turn rough ideas into validated designs stored as bd epics with immutable requirements; tasks created iteratively as you learn, not upfront."
- rigidity_level: HIGH FREEDOM — note the process order is fixed but Socratic questioning within steps adapts to context
- quick_reference: new 8-step table (Understanding, Exploring Approaches, Presenting Design, Architecture Impact Check, Creating Epic, Creating First Task, SRE Refinement, Handoff)
- when_to_use / don't use for
- the_process: detailed content for each of the 8 steps
  - Step 3 must include design-time friction trigger: detect awkwardness, shim language, pattern contradiction; offer /intuition with explicit wording template
  - Step 4 must list all 5 structural questions verbatim (from parent epic Requirements) and specify the route-to-/intuition behavior
  - Step 5 must embed the new epic template with "None because [reason]" example text in every optional-empty subsection (copy template verbatim from parent epic bd-5bg — do not paraphrase from memory)
- examples: 2-3 examples (the 3 preserved examples from current file; cut all thinking-mode examples)
- key_principles: preserved
- research_agents: preserved
- critical_rules: exactly 10 rules:
  1. Use AskUserQuestion tool
  2. Research BEFORE proposing
  3. Propose 2-3 approaches with trade-offs
  4. Epic requirements IMMUTABLE
  5. Include anti-patterns section
  6. Create ONLY first task
  7. Run SRE refinement before handoff
  8. Offer /intuition when design-time friction detected
  9. Architecture Impact Check required before epic finalization
  10. Subsection "None" requires grounded justification
- common_excuses: trim to match new rules (keep excuses for rules 1-10; drop all thinking-mode excuses)
- verification_checklist: concise single checklist (no mode split)
- integration: preserve references; add explicit BIDIRECTIONAL note about /intuition integration (both directions documented)
- resources: preserved

### 4. Embed new epic template in Step 5

Copy the template verbatim from parent epic bd-5bg. Required structure (top-level sections in order):
- Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- Approach
- Architecture
- Architecture Impact (with all 5 checkboxes and route-to-/intuition result format)
- Design Rationale (with 6 subsections: Problem, Research Findings, Key Decisions Made, Approaches Considered, Scope Boundaries, Open Questions)

Every subsection that could reasonably be empty must show a grounded-None example in the template (e.g., `External: None because this is a skill-framework refactor; no external libraries involved`).

### 5. Verify no forbidden terms present in new file

Run these greps on the new SKILL.md; all must return zero matches (case-insensitive where noted):
- `grep -i 'thinking mode' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md`
- `grep -i 'mode detection' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md`
- `grep 'Design Discovery' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md`
- `grep 'Architecture Context' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md`
- `grep 'Architecture Update Checklist' skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` (as an epic template section; concept still lives in review-implementation)

### 6. Bump plugin.json version

Read current version from .claude-plugin/plugin.json. Increment minor (e.g., 3.5.0 → 3.6.0), reset patch to 0. Do not guess current version — read it.

### 7. Pre-commit hook check

Run `pre-commit run --files skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md .claude-plugin/plugin.json` BEFORE final grep verification (markdown formatters may affect whitespace/headings). If auto-formatting occurs, re-run all grep checks from step 5.

### 8. Subagent read-test

Dispatch a general-purpose agent to read the new SKILL.md and report any ambiguous sections, missing cross-references, or incoherent steps. Treat agent feedback as blocking — address any critical ambiguities before closing the task.

## Key Considerations (added by SRE review)

### Edge Case: Pre-commit formatting interference
Pre-commit hooks may auto-format markdown (line wrapping, heading normalization). This could reorder or subtly break content.
Mitigation: Run pre-commit BEFORE final grep verification. If auto-formatting changes the file, re-run all grep checks.

### Edge Case: Load-bearing content not explicitly flagged
Something in the current file might be load-bearing even though it isn't in the preserve list above.
Mitigation: Before committing the new file, run `git diff skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md` and review any large deletions for unintended content loss. Keep git history accessible until validation is complete.

### Edge Case: Epic template drifts from parent epic spec
Two sources of truth (bd-5bg's Requirements + the embedded template in SKILL.md) risk divergence.
Mitigation: Copy the template verbatim from parent epic bd-5bg via `bd show bd-5bg`. Do not paraphrase from memory or reconstruct from conversation.

### Edge Case: Integration doc staleness via line numbers
Citations to intuition.md by line number break when intuition.md changes.
Mitigation: Cite intuition.md sections by name (e.g., "Step 4 Resolution Protocol") rather than line numbers.

### Edge Case: grep passes but content is incoherent
Grep verifications catch specific strings but not semantic coherence. A file could pass all greps while being unreadable.
Mitigation: Step 8 subagent read-test is required, not optional. Catch incoherence before closing.

### Edge Case: Version bump miscalculation
"Minor bump" is spec'd but the current version must be read, not assumed.
Mitigation: Use Read tool on .claude-plugin/plugin.json before modifying. Increment using actual current values.

## Anti-patterns (task-level)
- Do NOT partially rewrite. Either complete the rewrite or revert. Half-rewritten files leave seams and inconsistent rule lists.
- Do NOT introduce new terminology or sections not in the parent epic spec. Any addition requires explicit design review via conversation with user.
- Do NOT reintroduce any forbidden term (thinking mode, Design Discovery, Architecture Context, Architecture Update Checklist-as-template-section, mode detection) even in examples, comments, or integration notes.
- Do NOT skip the grep verification step. These greps are the forcing function that catches reintroduced terms.
- Do NOT skip the version bump. plugin.json versioning is mandatory per CLAUDE.md.
- Do NOT paraphrase the epic template from memory. Copy verbatim from parent epic bd-5bg.
- Do NOT skip pre-commit hooks. They catch formatting issues that could propagate.
- Do NOT skip the subagent read-test. Greps don't catch semantic incoherence.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md written with new 8-step process; no mode detection gate
- [ ] Zero matches on all five forbidden-term greps (thinking mode, mode detection, Design Discovery, Architecture Context, Architecture Update Checklist-as-template-section)
- [ ] Exactly 10 critical rules present in the new `<critical_rules>` block; count verified by numbering
- [ ] Three new rules present verbatim:
  - "Offer /intuition when design-time friction detected"
  - "Architecture Impact Check required before epic finalization"
  - "Subsection 'None' requires grounded justification"
- [ ] Epic template in Step 5 contains exactly 7 top-level sections in order (Requirements, Success Criteria, Anti-Patterns, Approach, Architecture, Architecture Impact, Design Rationale); verified by counting `## ` headings inside the template block
- [ ] Design Rationale in template has exactly 6 subsections (Problem, Research Findings, Key Decisions Made, Approaches Considered, Scope Boundaries, Open Questions); verified by counting `### ` headings under Design Rationale
- [ ] Step 4 contains all 5 Architecture Impact structural questions verbatim (same wording as parent epic Requirements) and documents /intuition routing
- [ ] Step 3 contains design-time friction trigger with explicit wording template (e.g., contains phrase "offer /intuition" or "run /intuition" in friction-detection context)
- [ ] Integration section explicitly documents BOTH directions: (a) brainstorm → /intuition on friction; (b) /intuition → brainstorm via Resolution Protocol "Brainstorm" option
- [ ] plugin.json version strictly greater than current by minor increment with patch reset to 0 (e.g., 3.5.0 → 3.6.0)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md and .claude-plugin/plugin.json
- [ ] Subagent read-test complete; agent reports zero critical ambiguities or incoherent sections

title: Feature: Wave-Based Team Execution Workflow
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Brainstorming skill produces epics with a Parallelism Map section identifying independent work streams, file ownership boundaries, and suggested wave composition
- A new wave-planning skill creates batches of parallelizable tasks from the Parallelism Map, sets beads dependencies, and runs SRE refinement on the batch
- A new team-executing-plans skill spawns agent teams from wave tasks, coordinates execution with hybrid delegation (Option C), reviews results, reconciles beads state, and plans next waves
- Agent definitions (test-runner, code-reviewer, codebase-investigator, internet-researcher) are upgraded with new subagent features (persistent memory, skills injection, permission modes, hooks)
- The using-hyper skill includes a decision point routing to team or solo execution path based on parallelism analysis
- Existing solo executing-plans skill continues to work unchanged
- STOP checkpoints occur at wave boundaries (not per-task) for human review
- Beads integration: teammates can safely read (bd show) and update status (bd update --status, bd close); lead handles task creation, dependency management, and sync

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Brainstorming skill epic template includes Parallelism Map section (independent streams, file ownership, suggested waves)
- [ ] wave-planning skill exists and creates task batches with beads dependencies from Parallelism Map
- [ ] team-executing-plans skill exists and spawns agent teams with hybrid delegation
- [ ] team-executing-plans presents wave-level STOP checkpoint summary after each wave
- [ ] Agent definitions use new subagent features (memory, permissionMode, skills, hooks, disallowedTools as appropriate)
- [ ] using-hyper routes to team path when 3+ independent streams exist, solo path otherwise
- [ ] Existing solo executing-plans skill works identically to before (no regressions)
- [ ] Teammate spawn prompts include: epic requirements, task details, anti-patterns, file ownership boundaries, TDD/verification methodology
- [ ] Lead operates in delegate mode during team execution (coordinate only, no implementation)
- [ ] Wave review includes: per-teammate results, integration test verification, beads reconciliation, next wave proposal

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO replacing the solo executing-plans workflow (coexist: team path is additive, solo path unchanged)
- ❌ NO teammates creating beads tasks or managing dependencies (lead manages task creation, bd dep add, bd create)
- ❌ NO teammates running bd sync (lead handles git sync at wave boundaries)
- ❌ NO skipping wave-level STOP checkpoints (human must review wave results before next wave spawns)
- ❌ NO spawning teams for fewer than 3 independent tasks (coordination overhead exceeds benefit; use solo path)
- ❌ NO teammates editing files outside their assigned ownership boundary (prevents merge conflicts)
- ❌ NO teammates messaging each other directly (all coordination flows through lead to maintain oversight)
- ❌ NO continuous multi-wave execution without human checkpoint (each wave boundary is a mandatory STOP)
- ❌ NO creating full task trees upfront (waves are created iteratively based on learnings from previous waves)

## Approach
Add a team-aware execution path to the hyperpowers workflow that coexists with the current solo path. The brainstorming skill gains a Parallelism Map section in its epic template that identifies independent work streams and file ownership. After brainstorming, a decision point routes to either the existing solo workflow (executing-plans) or the new team workflow (wave-planning → team-executing-plans). The team workflow creates waves of parallelizable tasks, spawns agent teams to execute them, reviews results at wave boundaries with mandatory human checkpoints, and iterates until all epic success criteria are met. Hybrid delegation (Option C) means teammates execute assigned tasks and can propose new tasks or flag issues to the lead, but the lead approves all changes to scope and dependencies.

## Architecture

### New Skills
- skills/wave-planning/SKILL.md - Analyzes Parallelism Map, creates task batches, sets dependencies, runs SRE refinement
- skills/team-executing-plans/SKILL.md - Spawns teams, monitors execution, reviews waves, reconciles beads, STOP checkpoints

### Modified Skills
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md - Add Parallelism Map section to epic template
- skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md - Add team vs. solo decision point and routing

### Modified Agents
- agents/test-runner.md - Add permissionMode: dontAsk, disallowedTools: Edit, Write
- agents/code-reviewer.md - Add memory: project, skills: [testing-anti-patterns]
- agents/codebase-investigator.md - Add memory: project, permissionMode: plan
- agents/internet-researcher.md - Add memory: user

### New Commands
- commands/execute-wave.md - Invokes team-executing-plans skill

### Workflow

```
Brainstorm → Epic (with Parallelism Map)
  → Decision: 3+ independent streams?
    → Yes: wave-planning → team-executing-plans → wave review loop → STOP
    → No: writing-plans → executing-plans (solo, unchanged)
  → review-implementation → finish-branch
```

### Teammate Spawn Template
Each teammate receives:
1. Epic requirements + anti-patterns (from bd show)
2. Specific task details (from bd show)
3. File ownership boundaries (from Parallelism Map)
4. Methodology skills (TDD, verification-before-completion)
5. Constraint: propose new work to lead, don't self-create tasks

### Beads Integration
- Before team spawn: bd ready identifies independent tasks, bd dep tree verifies ordering
- During execution: Teammates use bd update --status in_progress and bd close (safe via daemon RPC)
- After wave: Lead runs bd ready for unblocked work, creates new wave tasks, bd sync

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Hyperpowers executes tasks sequentially: one task → STOP → user reviews → next task. When an epic has 5+ tasks with independent work streams (different modules, different files), this leaves parallelism on the table. Claude Code's agent teams feature enables parallel execution by multiple Claude instances, but hyperpowers has no skill to leverage this.

### Research Findings

**Agent Teams (code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams):**
- Shared task list with file-locking for concurrent claiming
- Inter-agent messaging and teammate self-coordination
- Delegate mode restricts lead to coordination-only tools
- Plan approval requires teammate plans before implementing
- Experimental, higher token cost, works best when teammates operate independently

**Beads Concurrency (github.com/steveyegge/beads):**
- SQLite primary store with daemon singleton serializing all writes via RPC
- Three-layer locking: daemon lock, JSONL file lock, SQLite internal locking
- Hash-based IDs prevent cross-agent collisions
- Safe for concurrent CLI access when daemon is running
- Risk areas: note append (last-writer-wins), git push contention

**Subagent Evolution (code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents):**
- Persistent memory (user/project/local scope) for cross-session learning
- Skills injection via frontmatter for domain knowledge
- Hook support for validation within subagents
- Permission modes (plan, dontAsk, bypassPermissions)
- Context forking and backgrounding improvements

**ed3d-plugins (github.com/ed3dai/ed3d-plugins):**
