title: Brainstorming: LikeC4 MCP context loading + architecture update checklist
description: Replace bd-based architecture context loading in brainstorming building mode with LikeC4 MCP queries. Add architecture update checklist (5 questions) and dynamic view proposal to building mode flow. Builds on bd-2ub (mode detection gate) and bd-07n (LikeC4 model format).
design:
## Goal
Two changes to the brainstorming skill's building mode path:
1. Replace bd-based architecture context loading with LikeC4 MCP tool calls
2. Add architecture update checklist and dynamic view proposal to building mode flow

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Replace architecture context loading (building mode path)

The building mode path currently uses bd commands to detect and load architecture context (lines added by bd-2ub under BUILDING MODE section). Replace with LikeC4 MCP:

CURRENT (bd-based):
```bash
bd list --label arch --type epic --status open
bd list --label arch,component --parent <epic-id>
bd show <node-id>
bd dep tree <node-id>
```

NEW (LikeC4 MCP):
```
# Check for architecture model
ls arch/*.c4 2>/dev/null

# If found, use LikeC4 MCP to load context:
# 1. read-project-summary — get overview of element kinds, tags, elements
# 2. search-element — find component matching user's reference (by title, kind, tags, or metadata)
# 3. read-element — get full details: metadata (volatility_axis, layer, stability_state), relationships, source location
# 4. find-relationships — get adjacent components (direct and indirect)

# Still load ADRs the same way:
ls doc/arch/adr-*.md 2>/dev/null
# Read ADRs that mention the component or its volatility axis
```

Update the context presentation format:
```
Architecture context loaded for [component name]:
- Volatility axis: [from element metadata.volatility_axis]
- Layer: [from element metadata.layer]
- Stability state: [from element metadata.stability_state]
- Interface contract: [from linked doc/arch/components/<name>.md]
- Adjacent components: [from find-relationships — which components this one connects to]
- Relevant ADRs:
  - ADR-NNN: [title] — [key decision and consequences]
- Data flow context: [from dynamic views involving this component, via read-view]
  - Inbound: [from upstream in dynamic views]
  - Outbound: [to downstream in dynamic views]
  - Request paths: [dynamic views this component appears in]
```

If stability_state is 'pre-fit': note in presentation that volatility axis is a hypothesis.

### 2. Update codebase-investigator dispatch

The component-scoped investigator dispatch references bd deps for boundaries. Update to reference LikeC4 relationships:

CURRENT: 'For component [name] with these boundaries: [adjacent nodes from bd deps]'
NEW: 'For component [name] with these boundaries: [adjacent components from find-relationships]'

Reference the interface contract from doc/arch/components/<name>.md instead of bd design field.

### 3. Update edge cases section

Current edge cases reference bd (no architecture graph = no bd list results). Update:
- No architecture model exists (no arch/*.c4): skip detection, proceed with normal brainstorming
- No codebase but model exists: skip flow dispatch, present architectural context only
- Multiple components referenced: load context for all matched (search-element for each)
- Component is #target (aspirational): still load context, note it's aspirational
- No ADRs found: note 'No ADRs found for this component'
- User doesn't reference any component: skip detection, proceed normally
- LikeC4 MCP not running: error with 'LikeC4 MCP server required. Run: likec4 mcp --stdio'

### 4. Add architecture update checklist (end of building mode)

After Step 6 (SRE refinement + handoff), add a new note in the building mode flow:

```
## Architecture Update Note

After inner-loop work completes, the review-implementation skill runs this checklist:

1. Did this work change a component's public interface?
2. Did this work add or remove a dependency between components?
3. Did this work create a new component or remove an existing one?
4. Did this work move responsibility from one component to another?
5. Did this work create a new request path through 2+ components?

If any answer is 'yes':
- Update the relevant .c4 file(s)
- Remove #target tag if component now matches intent
- Update linked markdown doc if interface contract changed
- If Q5 yes, propose new dynamic view
- Confirm with architect before committing

This checklist is informational in the brainstorming epic design.
The review-implementation skill enforces it after execution.
```

### 5. Add dynamic view proposal during design

In Step 3 (Presenting the Design), add a question when the design involves data flowing through 2+ existing components:

'Does this feature create a new request path through 2+ existing components? If so, it may warrant a new dynamic view in arch/views/data-flows/.'

If yes, the epic's Architecture Context section should note: 'Proposed dynamic view: [flow name]'

### 6. Update epic design template

In Step 4 (Creating the bd Epic), add Architecture Context section to the epic template:

```
## Architecture Context
- Component: [LikeC4 element name]
- Volatility axis: [from metadata]
- Layer: [from metadata]
- Stability state: [from metadata]
- Adjacent: [from relationships]
- Relevant ADRs: [linked from element]
- Key data flows: [from dynamic views involving this component]
- Proposed dynamic view: [if new request path identified]
```

### 7. Test with subagents

Test cases:
- Building mode with arch/*.c4 present → loads via LikeC4 MCP (not bd)
- Building mode with no arch/ directory → skips context loading gracefully
- Building mode references specific component → search-element finds it, read-element loads details
- MCP not running → hard fail with helpful error message
- #target component referenced → context loaded, noted as aspirational
- Pre-fit component → volatility axis noted as hypothesis
- Feature creates new request path → dynamic view proposed
- Feature doesn't create new path → no dynamic view proposal
- Architecture update checklist present in epic template
- No bd architecture commands remain in building mode path (no bd list --label arch)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Building mode loads architecture context via LikeC4 MCP (search-element, read-element, find-relationships)
- [ ] No bd architecture commands remain (no bd list --label arch, no bd show for architecture nodes)
- [ ] MCP not running → hard fail with 'Run: likec4 mcp --stdio'
- [ ] ADR loading unchanged (still reads doc/arch/adr-*.md)
- [ ] Codebase-investigator dispatch references LikeC4 relationships (not bd deps)
- [ ] Architecture update checklist (5 questions) documented in building mode flow
- [ ] Dynamic view proposal question added to design presentation (Step 3)
- [ ] Epic template includes Architecture Context section
- [ ] Edge cases updated for LikeC4 (no arch/ dir, #target components, pre-fit, MCP down)
- [ ] Tested with subagents per writing-skills skill
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ Do NOT add .c4 file parsing as MCP fallback (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ Do NOT change thinking mode behavior (this task only touches building mode)
- ❌ Do NOT change Socratic questioning logic (only change context loading and epic template)
- ❌ Do NOT change mode detection gate from bd-2ub (additive only)
- ❌ Do NOT auto-insert ADR tradeoffs as anti-patterns (architect decides during brainstorm)

title: Rename architectural-audit to intuition — core rewrite with Brand framing
design:
## Goal
Rename skills/architectural-audit/ to skills/intuition/, rewrite SKILL.md with Brand's empirical framing, delete commands/audit-arch.md, create commands/intuition.md. This is the core rewrite — Pass 8, resolution protocol, forward audit, and model bootstrapping come in subsequent subtasks.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Rename skill directory
- mv skills/architectural-audit/ skills/intuition/

### Step 2: Rewrite SKILL.md section by section

**Frontmatter:**
- name: intuition
- description: Updated with Brand framing (empirical observation, shearing layers, git history as evidence)

**Overview:**
- Reframe from Hickey-only to Brand+Hickey: empirical observation of architecture via structured analysis
- Model describes what IS, not what should be

**Rigidity:**
- Update pass count from 7 to 7 (Pass 8 added in subtask B)
- Keep RIGID level

**Quick reference:**
- Step 0: Mode gate (Mode 1: model exists, Mode 2: no model)
- Steps 1-3: Updated pass descriptions without Löwy vocabulary

**When to use:**
- Remove '/decompose created or revised an architecture graph' — decompose is dead
- Add: 'When friction suggests structural mismatch', 'Periodic health check', 'When brainstorming suggests architectural investigation'
- Intuition is intermittent diagnostic, not part of sequential chain

**Don't use for:**
- Remove 'Creating a new decomposition (use /decompose)' — decompose is dead
- Remove 'When no architecture graph exists (run /decompose first)' — Mode 2 handles this now
- Keep: inner-loop feature work, bug fixes

**Step 0 (Mode gate):**
- Mode 1: Model exists (arch/*.c4 found) — load via LikeC4 MCP (existing behavior, updated vocabulary)
- Mode 2: No model exists — dispatch codebase-investigator for structure discovery, run passes against codebase evidence only
- Remove all #target handling (model describes what IS)
- Remove pre-fit component handling (Löwy concept)
- Remove 'run /decompose first' fallback
- Remove Löwy search terms (manager, engine, resourceAccessor, utility) from MCP queries
- Remove volatility_axis from metadata references
- Remove landscape view check referencing /decompose

**Step 1 (Gather evidence):**
- Keep codebase-investigator dispatch structure
- Remove Löwy layer references from investigation prompts
- Keep module structure, imports, co-change, call patterns, interfaces, shared state categories
- Keep Step 1b (data flow investigation)

**Passes 1-4, 6:**
- Remove Löwy vocabulary from all pass descriptions
- Remove #target handling from all passes
- Remove pre-fit component handling from all passes
- Keep Hickey simplicity framework (complection, interfaces, temporal coupling, hidden deps, state/identity)

**Pass 5 (reframed):**
- Was: 'Layer Violations' with Löwy layer rules (Manager/Engine/Resource Accessor/Utility)
- Now: 'Structural Dependency Direction' — dependencies flow from orchestration toward data/infrastructure
- Rule: upward calls from lower-abstraction to higher-abstraction modules are tensions
- Remove all Löwy layer names (Manager, Engine, Resource Accessor, Utility)
- Keep principle: one-way data flow, no upward dependencies

**Pass 7 (Dynamic View Drift):**
- Remove #target component skip
- Remove pre-fit component skip
- Keep dynamic view comparison logic

**Step 3 (Self-check + present):**
- Update report format: remove 'Target components (skipped)', remove 'Pre-fit components' sections
- Remove #target and pre-fit from model summary table
- Update resolution options: remove '/decompose to restructure' — replaced with 'ADR + bd ticket' (resolution protocol comes in subtask C, but remove /decompose now)
- Keep self-check procedure unchanged

**Examples:**
- Rewrite all examples to use Brand vocabulary
- Remove Löwy layer names from example components
- Remove #target and pre-fit from examples
- Remove /decompose references from examples
- Keep tension format and self-check demonstration

**Critical rules:**
- Remove rules about #target and pre-fit
- Remove references to /decompose
- Update pass count references
- Keep core rules: no recommendations, no severity, run all passes, self-check, etc.

**Verification checklist:**
- Remove #target and pre-fit checklist items
- Remove Löwy-specific items
- Add Mode 1/Mode 2 gate verification
- Keep core verification items

**Integration:**
- Update call chain to: /brainstorm → build → update model ↕ /intuition → find tensions → resolve → update model
- Remove /decompose from 'called by' and 'call chain'
- Update command reference from /audit-arch to /intuition
- Remove /decompose from artifacts consumed
- Intuition is intermittent diagnostic, not part of sequential chain

**Resources:**
- Remove Löwy layer rules section
- Keep Hickey framework references
- Update 'when stuck' section

### Step 3: Delete commands/audit-arch.md

### Step 4: Create commands/intuition.md
- description: Brand-framing description of empirical architecture observation
- Body: 'Use the hyperpowers:intuition skill exactly as written'

### Step 5: Verify
- grep -rn '#target' skills/intuition/SKILL.md returns zero
- grep -rn 'volatility.axis\|Manager.*Engine\|Resource.Accessor\|Utility' skills/intuition/SKILL.md returns zero (Löwy vocabulary)
- grep -rn '/decompose' skills/intuition/SKILL.md returns zero
- grep -rn 'pre-fit' skills/intuition/SKILL.md returns zero
- commands/audit-arch.md does not exist
- commands/intuition.md exists with correct content

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/architectural-audit/ does not exist
- [ ] skills/intuition/SKILL.md exists with Brand framing
- [ ] Mode 1 (model exists) and Mode 2 (no model) paths in Step 0
- [ ] Pass 5 reframed as structural dependency direction (no Löwy layer names)
- [ ] Zero #target references in skills/intuition/SKILL.md
- [ ] Zero Löwy vocabulary in skills/intuition/SKILL.md
- [ ] Zero /decompose references in skills/intuition/SKILL.md
- [ ] Zero pre-fit references in skills/intuition/SKILL.md
- [ ] commands/audit-arch.md does not exist
- [ ] commands/intuition.md exists with correct description
- [ ] Integration section shows revised call chain without /decompose
- [ ] When-to-use reflects intermittent diagnostic role
- [ ] All examples rewritten with Brand vocabulary
- [ ] Brand terms present: shearing layers, empirical observation, git history, rate of change

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO #target tag anywhere
- NO Löwy vocabulary as primary framing (volatility axis, Manager/Engine/Resource Accessor/Utility)
- NO /decompose references
- NO pre-fit component handling
- NO upfront force prediction language
- NO prescriptive resolution (tensions offer options, architect decides)
- NO automatic stability_state promotion

title: Create volatility-decomposition skill (SKILL.md + command)
design:
## Goal
Create the volatility-decomposition skill that guides architects through Löwy's volatility-based decomposition via Socratic dialogue, producing 2-3 alternative graph structures encoded in bd, with key decisions captured as ADRs.

## Effort Estimate
6-10 hours (documentation-heavy: ~400 line SKILL.md + examples + ADR template + command file)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing skill patterns
Read these files to internalize the structure and conventions:
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md — closest analog (Socratic, generative, produces bd artifacts). This is the primary reference.
- skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md — another skill that creates bd artifacts with detailed implementation steps
- commands/brainstorm.md — command file pattern (frontmatter + one-line instruction)
- skills/common-patterns/bd-commands.md — standard bd command examples to reference
- skills/common-patterns/common-anti-patterns.md — shared anti-patterns to reference
- skills/common-patterns/common-rationalizations.md — shared rationalizations to reference

### 2. Create skills/volatility-decomposition/SKILL.md

Follow the standard skill structure exactly:

```yaml
---
name: volatility-decomposition
description: Löwy volatility-based decomposition producing architecture graph in bd with ADRs
---
```

Required sections (in order):
1. `<skill_overview>` — 1-2 sentences: what and why
2. `<rigidity_level>` — HIGH FREEDOM (Socratic questions adapt to domain, but output structure is rigid: always 2-3 alternatives, always bd encoding, always ADRs)
3. `<quick_reference>` — table mapping steps to actions and deliverables
4. `<when_to_use>` — triggers: project inception, major inflection points, when structure fights you, fractal sub-decomposition of large components. NOT for: inner-loop feature work, bug fixes, refactoring
5. `<the_process>` — Steps 0-5 detailed below
6. `<examples>` — 4 examples detailed below
7. `<critical_rules>` — behavioral constraints
8. `<verification_checklist>` — evidence-based completion check
9. `<integration>` — caller/callee relationships and agents
10. `<resources>` — links to common patterns, Löwy references, ADR template

### 3. The Process — Step-by-step content

**Step 0 — Context detection:**
```bash
# Check for existing architecture epic
bd list --label arch --type epic --status open --json

# If found: load it + all component nodes (REVISION MODE)
bd show <epic-id> --json
bd list --label arch,component --parent <epic-id> --json

# If not found: INCEPTION MODE (starting fresh)
```
- If codebase exists: dispatch codebase-investigator with prompt: 'Map the current module/package structure, dependency graph between top-level modules, and any existing architectural boundaries. Stay at module level, not class/function level.'
- If no codebase: pure design mode — all evidence comes from Socratic dialogue

**Step 1 — Socratic questioning (Löwy framework):**
Use AskUserQuestion tool for ALL questions. Do not print questions and wait.

Question families (adapt to domain, but these are the core probes):

Forces discovery:
- 'What are the 3-5 major forces acting on this system? What pushes it to change?'
- 'Which of these forces are correlated (change together) vs independent?'

Volatility identification (per force):
- 'If you had to replace [X], what else would have to change?' (isolation test)
- 'How often does [X] change? Monthly? Yearly? Never?'
- 'Is this volatility (needs architectural encapsulation) or variability (handled with code)?'

Customer axis (Löwy's second dimension):
- 'Do different customers/user types need different [X]?'
- 'Will new customer segments require new behaviors here?'

Layer assignment:
- 'Is [component] orchestrating workflow (Manager), computing business logic (Engine), accessing data/external systems (Resource Accessor), or providing cross-cutting capability (Utility)?'
- Challenge: 'You said this is an Engine, but it accesses the database directly — should that be separated into a Resource Accessor?'

For REVISION MODE, shift to:
- 'What about the current structure feels wrong? Where is it fighting you?'
- 'If you restructure [X], what happens to its interfaces with [Y] and [Z]?'
- 'Does splitting [X] create genuinely independent axes, or will the halves still change together?'

**Step 2 — Structure proposal:**
Present 2-3 alternative decompositions. NEVER present just one.

Each proposal must include:
- Node list: name, responsibility (1 sentence), volatility axis (what it encapsulates), Löwy layer
- Edge list: which nodes have interface dependencies (blocks), which interact but don't block (relates_to)
- What this decomposition optimizes for (e.g., 'maximizes isolation of payment volatility')
- What it trades off (e.g., 'more components = more interfaces to maintain')

Format:
```
### Proposal A: [Name — what it optimizes for]

| Node | Responsibility | Volatility Axis | Layer |
|------|---------------|-----------------|-------|
| ... | ... | ... | Manager/Engine/RA/Utility |

Edges:
- [Node A] blocks [Node B] (interface: ...)
- [Node C] relates_to [Node D] (interaction: ...)

Optimizes for: [what]
Trades off: [what]

### Proposal B: [Name — what it optimizes for]
...
```

After presenting, ask: 'Which structure best matches your understanding of the system's volatility? You can also combine elements.'

**Step 3 — Encode in bd:**
After architect chooses:

```bash
# Create architecture epic
bd create '[System Name] Architecture' --type epic --label arch \
  --design '## System Purpose
[1-2 sentences from Socratic dialogue]

## Löwy Layer Map
- Manager: [components]
- Engine: [components]
- Resource Accessor: [components]
- Utility: [components]

## Global Constraints
[From Socratic dialogue — cross-cutting rules]'

# Create component nodes (one per component)
bd create '[Component Name]' --type feature --label arch,component \
  --parent <epic-id> \
  --design '## Volatility Axis
[What this component encapsulates — which force/change it isolates]

## Layer: [Manager|Engine|Resource Accessor|Utility]
[Why this layer — what it does and doesn't do]

## Interface Contract
- IN: [operations this component exposes]
- OUT: [operations this component calls on others]

## Responsibility
[1-2 sentences — what it does, what it does NOT do]'

# Set initial stability state
bd set-state <component-id> stability=exploring \
  --reason 'initial decomposition, not yet audited'

# Create edges
bd dep add <downstream> <upstream> --type blocks  # interface dependency
bd relate <node-a> <node-b>                        # non-blocking interaction
```

**Step 4 — Create ADRs:**
For each significant decomposition decision, guide the architect through creating an ADR.

The skill prompts the architect with a draft and asks for validation:
```
I'll draft an ADR for this decision. Review and I'll create the file:

# ADR-NNN: [Decision Title]

## Status
Accepted

## Context
[Forces from Socratic dialogue, neutral language]

## Decision
We will [active voice, full sentences].

## Consequences
- [Positive consequence]
- [Negative consequence / tradeoff]
- [What this enables or constrains for future work]
```

After architect approves, create the file using Write tool:
- Location: doc/arch/adr-NNN.md (create doc/arch/ directory if needed)
- Number sequentially (check existing ADRs first)
- ADRs to create: one per major boundary decision, one per significant tradeoff accepted

**Step 5 — Handoff guidance:**
```
## Graph Summary
[Table of nodes with stability status]

## Recommended Next Steps
- Run /audit-arch to stress-test this decomposition for hidden complection
- [List nodes that might already be stable candidates if simple/obvious]
- After audit: resolve or accept tensions, then stable nodes feed /brainstorm
```

### 4. Create commands/decompose.md
```markdown
---
description: Löwy volatility-based decomposition producing architecture graph in bd with ADRs
---

Use the hyperpowers:volatility-decomposition skill exactly as written
```

### 5. Create skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md
Content should include:
- Nygard format explanation (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences)
- Status values: proposed, accepted, deprecated, superseded
- Three example ADRs:
  1. Decomposition decision: 'We will isolate [X] because [volatility axis]'
  2. Tension resolution: 'We will [structural change] to resolve [tension]'
  3. Tension acceptance: 'We accept [tension] because [reasoning]. Revisit if [condition].'
- Guidance: consequences of one ADR become context for subsequent ADRs
- File naming: doc/arch/adr-NNN.md, sequential, never reuse numbers

## Examples to Write (4 required)

### Example 1: Good decomposition with clear volatility axes
- Scenario: Developer decomposes an order processing system
- Shows: proper Socratic questioning, 2-3 proposals, architect chooses, bd encoding, ADR creation
- Demonstrates: each node names its volatility axis, layer rules respected, interfaces explicit

### Example 2: BAD — Single recommendation instead of alternatives
- Scenario: Developer proposes one decomposition and says 'this is the right structure'
- Shows: why_it_fails (architect denied agency, missed better options, no tradeoff analysis)
- Correction: present 2-3 alternatives with tradeoffs, let architect choose

### Example 3: BAD — Non-independent volatility axes (functional decomposition in disguise)
- Scenario: Developer creates 'UserService', 'OrderService', 'ProductService' — organized by business function, not volatility
- Shows: why_it_fails (these are functional buckets, not volatility encapsulation — User and Order both change when 'adding subscription support')
- Correction: identify the actual independent axes (payment volatility, fulfillment volatility, pricing rules volatility) and encapsulate those

### Example 4: Good revision of an existing graph
- Scenario: After audit found tension, architect re-invokes decompose to restructure
- Shows: loading existing graph, targeted Socratic questions about the restructuring, proposing 2-3 revision options, updating bd nodes, creating ADR for the structural change

## Key Behavioral Constraints to Encode in Critical Rules
- ALWAYS present 2-3 alternatives, NEVER single recommendations
- Every node MUST name its volatility axis explicitly ('this encapsulates changes in [X]')
- Challenge when two nodes change for the same reason (independence violation)
- Challenge when interfaces carry more than one concern (leaky abstraction)
- Enforce Löwy layer rules: no upward deps, Managers have no business logic, Engines don't access resources directly
- Scale-agnostic vocabulary: adapt 'component/module/service' to context
- Think in components and boundaries, NOT classes and functions
- Distinguish volatility (architectural) from variability (code-level) — only volatility drives decomposition

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Pure Greenfield (no codebase, no existing graph)**
- Step 0 finds nothing — no epic, no codebase
- Skill must work entirely from Socratic dialogue
- All evidence comes from architect's domain knowledge
- Questions should be more exploratory: 'Tell me about this system. What does it do? Who uses it?'
- MUST NOT skip Step 0 and assume greenfield — always check first

**Edge Case: Revision Mode (existing graph)**
- Step 0 loads existing epic + nodes
- Socratic questions shift to: 'What feels wrong? Where is the structure fighting you?'
- Proposals are revisions, not replacements — may affect only 2-3 nodes
- Must update existing nodes, not create duplicates
- Use bd supersede if a node is being replaced, not just edited
- Create ADR for the structural change explaining why the revision was needed

**Edge Case: Architect Wants to Skip to Encoding**
- Architect says 'I already know the decomposition, just encode it'
- Skill should still ask key validation questions:
  - 'Can you name the volatility axis for each component?' (if they can't, the decomposition isn't volatility-based)
  - 'Are any two of these components likely to change for the same reason?' (independence check)
- If answers are solid, proceed to encoding. If not, push back gently.

**Edge Case: Architect Gives Minimal Answers**
- Some architects answer with one word or 'yes/no'
- Skill should provide concrete follow-up options via AskUserQuestion
- Never print open-ended questions and wait — always provide multiple-choice with an 'Other' option

**Edge Case: bd set-state Not Available**
- bd is evolving fast — set-state may not exist in all versions
- Fallback: use labels for stability tracking (label: arch:exploring, arch:stable)
- Document the fallback in the skill

**Edge Case: Fractal Sub-Decomposition**
- Architect runs /decompose on a component that's already in an existing architecture graph
- Must create sub-architecture within the existing node
- Sub-components are children of the component node (hierarchical IDs: bd-a3f8.1)
- Architecture of the sub-decomposition follows same rules (layers, volatility axes, ADRs)

## Anti-Patterns for This Task
- ❌ NO writing the skill from memory — must study brainstorming/SKILL.md first to match structure
- ❌ NO skipping examples — all 4 examples are required (skills without examples get ignored)
- ❌ NO vague example corrections — corrections must show exact corrected output
- ❌ NO placeholder text in any section ('[detailed above]', '[will be added]')
- ❌ NO writing the ADR template without concrete examples (abstract templates are useless)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] skills/volatility-decomposition/SKILL.md exists with complete skill structure (all 10 sections listed in Implementation step 2)
- [ ] commands/decompose.md exists with frontmatter + single-line invocation (matching commands/brainstorm.md pattern)
- [ ] skills/common-patterns/adr-template.md exists with Nygard format + 3 concrete example ADRs
- [ ] Process includes all 6 steps (0: context detection, 1: Socratic questioning, 2: structure proposal, 3: bd encoding, 4: ADR creation, 5: handoff guidance)
- [ ] Step 1 uses AskUserQuestion tool exclusively (never prints questions and waits)
- [ ] Step 2 presents exactly 2-3 alternatives with tradeoffs table format, NEVER a single recommendation
- [ ] Step 3 shows exact bd create commands with all flags (--type, --label, --parent, --design)
- [ ] Step 4 shows ADR drafting + Write tool file creation pattern
- [ ] All 4 examples written with scenario/code/why_it_fails/correction structure
- [ ] Example 3 specifically demonstrates functional decomposition anti-pattern (organized by business function, not volatility)
- [ ] Critical rules section encodes all 8 behavioral constraints listed above
- [ ] Verification checklist includes: 'every node names its volatility axis', 'all alternatives presented', 'ADRs created for key decisions'
- [ ] Edge cases documented: greenfield, revision mode, skip-to-encoding, minimal answers, bd fallback, fractal sub-decomposition
- [ ] Skill tested with subagent: give subagent scenario 'decompose a notification system for a SaaS product', verify it produces 2-3 alternatives (not 1), encodes in bd format, and drafts an ADR
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

title: Task 1: Restructure executor commit flow and message format
design:
## Goal
Modify agents/executor.md to move commit from per-deliverable (inside TDD cycle) to per-task (gate before bd close), and update the task completion message format to include commit hashes.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours (markdown-only edits to a single file)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- agents/executor.md:58-91 — TDD cycle section ("2. Execute with TDD") containing Steps 1-6
- agents/executor.md:83-91 — Step 6 (Commit) inside TDD cycle — THIS IS WHAT WE REMOVE
- agents/executor.md:93-98 — Section "3. Verify completeness"
- agents/executor.md:100-106 — Section "4. Close the task" — commit gate goes BEFORE this
- agents/executor.md:131-159 — Message Protocol > Task Completion Message — add Commits section here
- agents/executor.md:242-261 — "Rules (No Exceptions)" section — add anti-skip rule here

### 2. Remove commit from TDD cycle
In the "2. Execute with TDD" section (line 56), remove Step 6 (Commit) at lines 83-91. The TDD cycle becomes Steps 1-5 only:
- Step 1 — Write failing test (RED) (line 60)
- Step 2 — Run test, confirm FAIL (line 63)
- Step 3 — Implement minimal code (GREEN) (line 70)
- Step 4 — Run test, confirm PASS (line 73)
- Step 5 — Refactor (line 80)

NOTE: These are TDD steps nested inside Execution Loop section "2. Execute with TDD". Do NOT confuse with Execution Loop top-level sections (1. Plan substeps, 2. Execute with TDD, 3. Verify completeness, etc.).

Also update the introductory text at line 13 which says "red-green-refactor-commit cycle" — change to "red-green-refactor cycle" since commit is no longer per-deliverable.

### 3. Add commit as gate before bd close
Between section "3. Verify completeness" (lines 93-98) and section "4. Close the task" (lines 100-106), insert a new section. Renumber: current "4. Close the task" becomes "5. Close the task", current "5. Propose next task" becomes "6. Propose next task".

New section to insert:

### 4. Commit

Commit all work for this task. This is MANDATORY — NEVER run bd close without committing first.

```bash
git add <specific files changed in this task>
git commit -m "<descriptive message summarizing all task work>

bd: <task-id>"
```

**CRITICAL: Committing is a prerequisite to task closure. If you skip the commit, work is not saved and may be lost. NEVER rationalize skipping — not "I'll commit later", not "finish-branch will handle it", not "it's just a small change".**

### 4. Update task completion message format
In the Message Protocol section (lines 131-159), update the Task Completion Message format to include a Commits section between Done and Learned:

```
## Task <id> Complete

### Done
- [1-3 bullet summary of what was implemented]
- [Key files created or modified]

### Commits
- [short hash]: [commit message summary]

### Learned
- [Discoveries that affect future tasks]
- [Things that differed from assumptions]
- [Or "None — executed as planned"]

### Changed from plan
- [What deviated from the task description and why]
- [Or "None — executed as planned"]

### Proposed next task
Title: [task title]
Goal: [what it delivers — one clear outcome]
Approach: [how to implement, informed by learnings from this task]
SRE refined: yes
Key considerations: [corner cases identified by SRE refinement]
```

### 5. Add anti-skip rule
In the "Rules (No Exceptions)" section starting at line 242, add a new numbered rule:

10. **Always commit before closing a task.** Run git add and git commit before bd close. NEVER defer commits to "later" or "finish-branch". Each task's work must be committed before closure. Include the commit hash(es) in your task completion message to the lead.

## Anti-Patterns (Task-Specific)
- ❌ Do NOT change TDD cycle behavior (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR stays exactly the same)
- ❌ Do NOT change startup protocol, escalation triggers, completion protocol, or any behavior other than commit timing
- ❌ Do NOT add commit back into the TDD cycle — it goes ONLY before bd close
- ❌ Do NOT use generic commit messages — message must describe all task work

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Step 6 (Commit) at lines 83-91 removed from TDD cycle section
- [ ] Line 13 intro text updated from "red-green-refactor-commit" to "red-green-refactor"
- [ ] New section "4. Commit" added between Verify completeness and Close task
- [ ] Sections after the new Commit section renumbered (Close=5, Propose=6)
- [ ] Commit step has explicit anti-skip language with specific rationalizations called out
- [ ] Task completion message format (lines 137-159) includes ### Commits section between Done and Learned
- [ ] New rule #10 added to "Rules (No Exceptions)" section about mandatory commit before close
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

title: Task 5 (final): Update /ponder Mode cross-refs, bump plugin version, run epic verification
description: Final task. Update /ponder/SKILL.md's 6 stale Mode 1/Mode 2 cross-references to /intuition; bump .claude-plugin/plugin.json from 3.7.0 to 3.8.0; run epic-level success-criteria verification. CLAUDE.md and README.md surveyed clean (0 matches); no edits needed there.
design:
## Goal
Final cleanup task. Update skills/ponder/SKILL.md's 6 stale Mode 1/Mode 2 cross-references to /intuition (lines 53, 124, 212, 232, 258, 392) — these refer to /intuition's old Mode 1/Mode 2 terminology that no longer exists. Bump .claude-plugin/plugin.json minor version (3.7.0 → 3.8.0). Run final epic-level success-criteria verification across all 16 boxes from epic bd-wxj.

CLAUDE.md and README.md are already clean (0 matches for Mode 1/Mode 2/--scope/--epic/10 passes/Pass 7|10 — surveyed by lead before this task). Do not touch them.

## This task does NOT touch
- skills/intuition/SKILL.md (already trimmed in bd-428, bd-bkv, bd-5p1)
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (already updated in bd-ufl)
- agents/ponder.md (no changes needed)
- CLAUDE.md (already clean — surveyed)
- README.md (already clean — surveyed)
- /ponder's own internal mode terminology (UPDATE / BOOTSTRAP / REVIEW are /ponder's own mode names; do NOT change those)

## This task DOES touch
- skills/ponder/SKILL.md (6 cross-references to /intuition's old Mode 1/Mode 2 terminology)
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (version bump)

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN in this task)
- ❌ NO touching /ponder's own mode names (UPDATE / BOOTSTRAP / REVIEW). These are /ponder's internal terminology, not /intuition's deprecated Mode 1/Mode 2.
- ❌ NO version bump skipped (saved feedback: "ALWAYS bump plugin.json version before final push; user has had to remind multiple times")
- ❌ NO regressing prior trims (run full epic verification at end to catch regressions)
- ❌ NO unilateral trimming to hit ~1280 line target — current is 1391 lines (221 saved vs 332 target). The picked trims yielded 221; do NOT add new trims not in scope. Note the gap in the completion report; reviewer/architect decides whether to extend the epic.
- ❌ NO partial commits (commit only when all changes verified)
- ❌ NO bumping major version (minor only: 3.7.0 → 3.8.0)
- ❌ NO touching CLAUDE.md or README.md (already clean per lead survey)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

- Read skills/ponder/SKILL.md lines 50-60, 120-130, 210-235, 255-265, 388-395 (the 6 specific Mode 1/Mode 2 references to understand context)
- Read .claude-plugin/plugin.json (current version 3.7.0)
- Read epic bd-wxj success criteria (16 boxes) to plan final verification

### 2. Write tests first (TDD — RED phase)

Save as /tmp/verify-bd-task5.sh, chmod +x, run pre-edit (must FAIL) then post-edit (must PASS):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

P=skills/ponder/SKILL.md
J=.claude-plugin/plugin.json
I=skills/intuition/SKILL.md
B=skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md

echo "=== /ponder: stale Mode 1/Mode 2 cross-refs to /intuition removed ==="
! grep -E '\bMode 1\b' "$P" || { echo "FAIL: 'Mode 1' phrase still in /ponder"; exit 1; }
! grep -E '\bMode 2\b' "$P" || { echo "FAIL: 'Mode 2' phrase still in /ponder"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /ponder: own UPDATE/BOOTSTRAP/REVIEW mode names preserved ==="
grep -E 'UPDATE mode|UPDATE:' "$P" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: /ponder UPDATE mode terminology missing"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'BOOTSTRAP mode|BOOTSTRAP:' "$P" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: /ponder BOOTSTRAP mode terminology missing"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'REVIEW mode|REVIEW:' "$P" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: /ponder REVIEW mode terminology missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== plugin.json: version bumped to 3.8.0 ==="
grep -F '"version": "3.8.0"' "$J" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: plugin.json version not bumped to 3.8.0"; exit 1; }
! grep -F '"version": "3.7.0"' "$J" || { echo "FAIL: plugin.json still on 3.7.0"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /intuition: prior trim work intact (regression checks) ==="
! grep -E '\bMode [12]\b' "$I" || { echo "FAIL: Mode 1/Mode 2 reappeared in /intuition"; exit 1; }
! grep -F -- '--scope' "$I" || { echo "FAIL: --scope reappeared"; exit 1; }
! grep -F 'Dynamic View Drift' "$I" || { echo "FAIL: Pass 7 content reappeared in /intuition"; exit 1; }
grep -E 'Run ALL 9 passes' "$I" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: 'Run ALL 9 passes' missing"; exit 1; }
! grep -F 'Mode: BOOTSTRAP' "$I" || { echo "FAIL: BOOTSTRAP template reappeared in /intuition"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'Optional: Prose Focus' "$I" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: Prose Focus subsection missing"; exit 1; }
grep -F 'NO recommendations' "$I" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: rule 1 missing"; exit 1; }

echo "=== /brainstorm: prose-focus update intact ==="
grep -E 'prose focus' "$B" > /dev/null || { echo "FAIL: brainstorm prose-focus reference missing"; exit 1; }
! grep -F -- '--scope' "$B" || { echo "FAIL: brainstorm has --scope flag"; exit 1; }

echo "All Task 5 checks passed."
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Section A: Update /ponder Mode cross-references**

For each of the 6 lines, REPLACE the stale Mode 1/Mode 2 reference with /intuition's current terminology:

- [ ] skills/ponder/SKILL.md:53 — `Intuition Step 4 first Resolve on Mode 2 audit` → `Intuition Step 4 first Resolve on audit without architecture model`
- [ ] skills/ponder/SKILL.md:124 — `Note: "Future audits will run in Mode 1"` → `Note: "Future audits will load the model"`
- [ ] skills/ponder/SKILL.md:212 — `Intuition Step 4: architect resolves first tension with "Resolve" on Mode 2 audit.` → `Intuition Step 4: architect resolves first tension with "Resolve" on audit without architecture model.`
- [ ] skills/ponder/SKILL.md:232 — `Source: Intuition Mode 2 audit findings + architect input` → `Source: Intuition audit (no architecture model) findings + architect input`
- [ ] skills/ponder/SKILL.md:258 — `Architecture model bootstrapped. Future audits will run in Mode 1.` → `Architecture model bootstrapped. Future audits will load the model.`
- [ ] skills/ponder/SKILL.md:392 — `Step 4: first Resolve on Mode 2 → /ponder bootstrap → model created → future Mode 1` → `Step 4: first Resolve on audit without model → /ponder bootstrap → model created → future audits load the model`

NOTE: Line numbers may have shifted slightly — use grep `\bMode [12]\b skills/ponder/SKILL.md` to find current locations if line refs are off. Do NOT update /ponder's own UPDATE/BOOTSTRAP/REVIEW mode names — those are /ponder's internal terminology.

**Section B: Bump plugin version**

- [ ] Read .claude-plugin/plugin.json
- [ ] Edit `"version": "3.7.0"` → `"version": "3.8.0"` (minor bump; epic adds prose-focus mechanism + consolidates ownership which are user-facing changes)

**Section C: Final epic-level verification**

Run `bd show bd-wxj` and verify each success criterion:

- [ ] Run all prior tasks' verification scripts (or equivalent grep checks) to confirm no regressions:
  - `bash /tmp/verify-bd-428.sh` (Mode Gate, scope, freshness, prose focus)
  - `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task2.sh` (Pass 7, renumber)
  - `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task3.sh` (Bootstrap simplification)
  - `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task4.sh` (brainstorm prose focus)
  - `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task5.sh` (this task)
- [ ] Verify each of the 16 epic success criteria. For criteria already verified by prior task scripts, note "verified via task verification". For novel checks (e.g., line count vs target), note actual value:
  - [ ] /intuition no Mode 1/Mode 2 — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-428.sh
  - [ ] /intuition no --scope/--epic — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-428.sh
  - [ ] /intuition has 9 passes — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-task2.sh
  - [ ] /intuition documents Optional Prose Focus — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-428.sh
  - [ ] /intuition no Model Freshness check — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-428.sh
  - [ ] /intuition Step 4 Resolve no longer templates /ponder bootstrap input — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-task3.sh
  - [ ] Pass 7 logic codified in /ponder REVIEW — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-task2.sh
  - [ ] /brainstorm uses prose focus — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-task4.sh
  - [ ] Pass references renumbered — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-task2.sh
  - [ ] Cross-references explicit — verify via this task (after /ponder Mode refs updated)
  - [ ] Anti-recommendation rules verbatim — verified via every prior task verification
  - [ ] Resolution Protocol unchanged — manual spot-check in this task
  - [ ] Architect Questions unchanged — manual spot-check in this task
  - [ ] CLAUDE.md / README.md updated — note: lead survey showed both already clean; no edits needed; criterion considered met
  - [ ] plugin.json version bumped — verified via /tmp/verify-bd-task5.sh
  - [ ] Reduces /intuition SKILL.md by ~300+ lines — actual: 221 lines (1612 → 1391). Below target (~330) but the picked trims were all completed; further reduction would require unscoped changes.

**Section D: Report final line count and any open concerns in completion report**

- [ ] Note: actual /intuition line count is ~1391 (started 1612). Reduction is 221 lines, which is below the "~300+ lines" target. The trims explicitly picked by the architect during brainstorm yielded this; no additional in-scope trims remain.
- [ ] If reviewer raises this gap, options are: (a) accept reality and revise the criterion; (b) extend the epic with a follow-up task to find more trims; (c) close the epic with this gap noted.

**Section E: Pre-commit + verify**

- [ ] Run `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task5.sh` against pre-edit state; confirm it FAILS (RED phase).
- [ ] Make edits per Sections A-B.
- [ ] Run `bash /tmp/verify-bd-task5.sh` against post-edit state; confirm "All Task 5 checks passed."
- [ ] Run all prior task verification scripts; confirm no regressions.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass.
- [ ] Commit (single commit; do not amend).

## Success Criteria

**Verification grep checks (must all pass):**
- [ ] `! grep -E '\bMode 1\b' skills/ponder/SKILL.md` succeeds (no /intuition Mode 1 cross-ref)
- [ ] `! grep -E '\bMode 2\b' skills/ponder/SKILL.md` succeeds (no /intuition Mode 2 cross-ref)
- [ ] `grep -E 'UPDATE mode' skills/ponder/SKILL.md` succeeds (own mode names preserved)
- [ ] `grep -E 'BOOTSTRAP mode' skills/ponder/SKILL.md` succeeds
- [ ] `grep -E 'REVIEW mode' skills/ponder/SKILL.md` succeeds
- [ ] `grep -F '"version": "3.8.0"' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` succeeds
- [ ] `! grep -F '"version": "3.7.0"' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` succeeds

**Regression checks (all prior task verifications must still pass):**
- [ ] /intuition: no Mode 1/Mode 2, no --scope/--epic, no Dynamic View Drift, "Run ALL 9 passes" present, no Mode: BOOTSTRAP template, Optional: Prose Focus present, NO recommendations rule present
- [ ] /brainstorm: prose focus referenced, no --scope flag

**Manual checks:**
- [ ] Resolution Protocol Step 4 in /intuition still has 5 options (Accept, Resolve, Brainstorm, Investigate, Skip)
- [ ] Architect Questions in /intuition Step 3 still present (Meadows / Alexander / Rao)
- [ ] Anti-recommendation self-check rules 1-4 in /intuition Critical Rules verbatim
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

**Final epic verification (report results in completion):**
- [ ] All 16 epic success criteria verified (or noted with status if not strictly met)
- [ ] Line count gap noted: 1391 actual vs ~1280 target; 221 line reduction vs ~330 target

## Edge Cases & Failure Modes

**/ponder's own UPDATE/BOOTSTRAP/REVIEW mode names accidentally edited:**
- The Mode 1/Mode 2 grep patterns (`\bMode [12]\b`) deliberately use word boundaries and digit constraints to avoid matching /ponder's own UPDATE/BOOTSTRAP/REVIEW. Verify that the renumber-style verification doesn't accidentally match these.

**Line numbers shifted from prior survey:**
- The lead surveyed at lines 53, 124, 212, 232, 258, 392 BEFORE this task. After other commits in this epic, line numbers may have shifted. Use grep to find current locations rather than relying on line numbers.

**Major version desired instead of minor:**
- Per epic requirements: "minor bump". Do NOT bump major (3.7.0 → 4.0.0). 3.8.0 is correct.

**Reviewer flags line-count gap:**
- Anticipated. Document the gap clearly in the completion report. The picked trims yielded 221 lines; reaching ~330 would require additional in-scope trims. Architect can decide.

**CLAUDE.md or README.md actually have references that the survey missed:**
- Re-grep them in this task as a sanity check. Survey was: `grep -cE '\bMode [12]\b|--scope|--epic|10 passes|Pass (7|10)\b'` → both 0. If repeat survey shows new matches, address them as in-scope; otherwise the criterion "CLAUDE.md and README.md mentions of /intuition modes/scopes updated" is met by absence (nothing to update).

**Concurrent edits:**
- Single-author refactor; no concurrency concerns.

## Notes for next task
After this task completes, the epic is done (or has a documented line-count gap). The lead should dispatch the reviewer agent for final verification.

If line-count gap is unacceptable to architect: a follow-up task could find additional trims (most likely candidates: Pass 6 State/Identity, Pass description verbosity, Examples compression). But that decision belongs to the architect.

description: Final task for epic bd-6uw. Three coordinated cleanups: bump plugin.json 3.8.0→3.8.1, remove sre-task-refinement from agents/executor.md skills frontmatter (flagged as follow-up in bd-3nj learnings), run cross-file consistency grep across agents/skills/commands/docs to verify no stale executor-side SRE references remain. design:
## Goal

Final task for epic bd-6uw. Three coordinated cleanups:
(a) Bump `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` version 3.8.0 → 3.8.1.
(b) Remove `- sre-task-refinement` from the `skills:` list in `agents/executor.md` frontmatter (line 10) — the new Rule 4 forbids running SRE in executor; the skill should not be auto-loaded into executor context. Flagged as follow-up by bd-3nj learnings.
(c) Run cross-file consistency grep across `agents/`, `skills/`, `commands/`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `README.md` for any remaining stale references to executor-side SRE (instructions to run SRE inside executor, references to the removed "Run SRE refinement on proposed next task" step, `SRE refined: yes` template lines outside intentional historical refs). Fix coherence breaks; leave intentional mentions (e.g., the new lead-side rule, the negative `Do NOT run` rule, learnings-file historical content).

This task closes the epic. After commit, all epic success criteria should be met and the lead dispatches the reviewer agent.

## Implementation

### 1. Pre-edit Reads (MANDATORY first step)

Read all files in scope before editing — drift check:
- `Read .claude-plugin/plugin.json` — verify version field is `"version": "3.8.0"`.
- `Read agents/executor.md` lines 1-15 — verify line 10 reads `  - sre-task-refinement` and is part of the `skills:` YAML list.
- Skim `agents/reviewer.md`, `commands/execute-plan.md`, `commands/brainstorm.md`, `commands/write-plan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md` mentally — large files, just confirm they exist and aren't unexpectedly different.

If material drift, return ESCALATION.

### 2. Implementation checklist (3 deliverables)

#### Deliverable 1: plugin.json version bump

- [ ] **RED:** `grep -n '"version": "3.8.0"' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` — must return exactly one match.
- [ ] **Edit:** Replace `"version": "3.8.0"` with `"version": "3.8.1"` in `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`. Use Edit tool; the line is unique.
- [ ] **GREEN:** `grep -n '"version": "3.8.1"' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` — must return exactly one match.
- [ ] **GREEN:** `grep -n '"version": "3.8.0"' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` — must return ZERO matches.

#### Deliverable 2: Remove sre-task-refinement from executor.md skills frontmatter

- [ ] **RED:** `sed -n '7,12p' agents/executor.md` — must show the YAML `skills:` block including `- sre-task-refinement` line.
- [ ] **Edit:** Remove the line `  - sre-task-refinement` from `agents/executor.md` (currently line 10). Use Edit with sufficient `old_string` context — include the surrounding skills list lines for uniqueness:
  ```
  skills:
    - test-driven-development
    - verification-before-completion
    - sre-task-refinement
  ---
  ```
  Replace with:
  ```
  skills:
    - test-driven-development
    - verification-before-completion
  ---
  ```
- [ ] **GREEN:** `grep -n "sre-task-refinement" agents/executor.md` — must return ZERO matches (frontmatter line gone; the prior occurrence in Rule 4 used the prose "SRE refinement" not the skill identifier — verify by reading Rule 4 line).
- [ ] **GREEN:** `grep -nE "^  - (test-driven-development|verification-before-completion)" agents/executor.md` — must return exactly two matches (the remaining skills).

#### Deliverable 3: Cross-file consistency grep

Scan key files for stale executor-side SRE references. The scope is intentionally narrow — fix only coherence breaks (instructions/references that contradict the new behavior); leave historical or intentional mentions.

- [ ] **Run:** `grep -rn "Run SRE refinement on proposed next task" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` — must return ZERO matches (the removed Step 2 is gone; no other file should reference it).
- [ ] **Run:** `grep -rn "SRE refined: yes" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` — must return ZERO matches in current docs (may match historical learnings files outside this scope — those are fine).
- [ ] **Run:** `grep -rn "executor.*runs SRE\|executor runs sre-task-refinement" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` — review every match. Each must be:
  - A NEGATIVE statement (executor does NOT run SRE — fine, leave) OR
  - In the new lead-side critical rule context (lead dispatches; executor doesn't — fine, leave) OR
  - A learnings-file reference (out of scope — these are in the memory dir, not in the repo).
  If any match is a POSITIVE instruction for executor to run SRE, it's stale — edit to fix.
- [ ] **Run:** `grep -rn "Never skip SRE refinement" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` — must return ZERO matches.
- [ ] **Run:** `grep -rn "Key considerations: \\[corner cases identified by SRE refinement\\]" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` — must return ZERO matches.

If any of the above grep checks flags a stale reference: edit the file to align with the new behavior. Use the test-runner agent for grep execution to keep verbose output out of context.

If all greps return as expected (no stale references found): record this fact in the learnings file.

### 3. Commit

Single commit covering all 3 deliverables. Include any consistency-fix files in the commit. Message:
```
chore(bd-6uw): bump version to 3.8.1, drop sre-task-refinement from executor skills

bd: <task-id>

Final task for epic bd-6uw (executor format drift + context exhaustion fix).
```

Stage exactly: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, `agents/executor.md`, plus any files modified by Deliverable 3 cross-file consistency fixes.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` version is `3.8.1`.
- [ ] `agents/executor.md` skills frontmatter no longer includes `- sre-task-refinement`.
- [ ] `agents/executor.md` skills frontmatter still includes `- test-driven-development` and `- verification-before-completion`.
- [ ] `grep -rn "Run SRE refinement on proposed next task" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` returns ZERO matches.
- [ ] `grep -rn "SRE refined: yes" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` returns ZERO matches.
- [ ] `grep -rn "Never skip SRE refinement" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` returns ZERO matches.
- [ ] `grep -rn "Key considerations: \\[corner cases identified by SRE refinement\\]" agents/ skills/ commands/ CLAUDE.md README.md` returns ZERO matches.
- [ ] All POSITIVE "executor runs SRE" instructions removed (verified via the targeted grep — if any matches were found in Deliverable 3, they were fixed).
- [ ] Single commit on this task with all changes staged.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass.

## Key Considerations

### Pre-Edit File-Drift Check (Same as bd-3nj/bd-uac)

Mandatory pre-edit Reads to confirm file structure. Escalate on material drift.

### Editing YAML Frontmatter

The skills list in `agents/executor.md` is YAML. Removing one item requires careful attention to indentation (2 spaces under `skills:`) and the closing `---`. The Edit `old_string` should include the surrounding skills entries plus the closing `---` to ensure uniqueness; `new_string` is the same minus the `- sre-task-refinement` line.

### Consistency Grep Scope Discipline

The cross-file consistency check is INTENTIONALLY narrow. Anti-pattern: scope creep into "let me also clean up other unrelated SRE references I see." Stick to the 5 specific grep checks. If the executor finds matches that are ambiguous (e.g., a doc mentions SRE refinement in passing without instructing executor to run it), default to LEAVE; consistency repair is for clear coherence breaks only.

### Learnings File Notation

If Deliverable 3's greps all return zero (no stale references found anywhere), record this explicitly in `epic-bd-6uw-task-bd-fjf-learnings.md` (or whatever bd-id this task gets): "Cross-file consistency clean — no stale executor-side SRE references in agents/, skills/, commands/, CLAUDE.md, or README.md." This is useful context for the reviewer agent.

If matches WERE found and fixed, record:
- File path + line where the stale reference lived
- Original text vs. fixed text
- Why it was a coherence break (instruction conflicting with new behavior)

### Inherited Anti-Patterns (Reminder)

From epic bd-6uw, these apply to this task:
- ❌ NO PostToolUse hooks (epic-level constraint).
- ❌ NO modifying envelope shapes (already shipped; do not touch).
- ❌ NO removing TaskCreate sub-step tracking.
- ❌ NO running SRE in both executor and lead — moves to lead only.
- ❌ NO token introspection logic.
- ❌ NO further root-cause spelunking.
- ❌ NO scope expansion to other agents (reviewer, ponder, etc.) — even if you find SRE-related comments in those files, leave them. They are separate concerns.

### Final Output Contract Reminder

Per the new "Final Output Contract" at top of agents/executor.md (already shipped in bd-3nj), your final response MUST start with `## Status: COMPLETE` and contain Task / Done / Commits / Learned / Changed from plan / Proposed next task sections. Run the Verify Final Output GATE in your Completion Protocol before returning.

Since this is the FINAL task in the epic, your COMPLETE envelope should additionally indicate "Epic Status: All success criteria met. Ready for reviewer dispatch." per the Epic Completion Report format in agents/executor.md. Include a Success Criteria Evidence section.

## Notes for executor

This task closes epic bd-6uw. After your COMPLETE envelope, the lead dispatches the reviewer agent — do NOT close the epic yourself. Only close the individual task (bd close <task-id>).

The proposed next task in your COMPLETE envelope should be: "None — epic complete; lead dispatches reviewer." Or omit the Proposed next task section since the Epic Completion Report format takes precedence for final tasks.
notes:
## SRE Review Addendum (added 2026-04-28)

### Ambiguity Escalation Protocol (Deliverable 3)

The original task said 'default to LEAVE' on ambiguous Deliverable 3 grep matches. Tightening this to prevent rationalized skip-thinking:

- **0 ambiguous matches** (all 5 grep checks clean): proceed; record clean state in learnings.
- **1-2 ambiguous matches** (executor uncertain whether reference is stale or intentional): document each match in learnings file with file:line + 1-sentence judgment + chosen action (LEAVE or FIX). LEAVE is acceptable for clearly historical/changelog content; FIX is required for any positive instruction to executor to run SRE.
- **3+ ambiguous matches** OR **any match the executor genuinely cannot judge**: return ESCALATION. The lead reviews each match against epic intent before approving the task close.

Rationale: 'default to LEAVE' enables 'eh, probably fine' rationalization. The tiered protocol forces explicit judgment per match (1-2) and explicit lead review when there's significant ambiguity (3+), without re-dispatching for trivial single-match cases.

### YAML Frontmatter Parsing (Deliverable 2)

Removing  from the skills list does not require special YAML handling. The Claude Code agent loader treats the list as unordered set of skill names; remaining order is preserved naturally; no quoting issues. The  closing fence after the list stays as-is.

### Approved by SRE Review

All 8 categories applied. Granularity ✅. Implementability ✅. Success Criteria ✅. Dependencies ✅. Safety ✅. Edge Cases ✅ (file drift, YAML editing, ambiguity protocol now explicit). Red Flags ✅ (no placeholders). Test Meaningfulness ✅.

title: Rewrite dispatch model in executing-plans and executor agent
design:
## Goal
Rewrite the two primary files to switch from teammate (SendMessage-based) dispatch to blocking subagent (return-based) dispatch. These must change together as they define both sides of the interface contract.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (documentation rewrite, two files)

## Implementation

### 1. Rewrite agents/executor.md

**Remove:**
- All SendMessage references (completion report, escalation, progress, epic completion report)
- All 'team-lead' references
- All 'wait for lead' instructions
- All 'idles out naturally' language (subagents return, they don't idle)
- Frontmatter description referencing TEAMMATE and team_name
- Epic Completion Report section's SendMessage usage (section starting at current line 282)

**Add — Structured return format:**
The executor's final output follows one of three formats:

```
## Status: COMPLETE

### Task: <task-id>
### Done
- [1-3 bullet summary]
- [Key files created or modified]
### Commits
- [short hash]: [commit message summary]
### Learned
- [Discoveries that affect future tasks]
### Changed from plan
- [Deviations and why]
### Proposed next task
Title: [task title]
Goal: [what it delivers]
Approach: [how to implement]
SRE refined: yes
Key considerations: [corner cases from SRE refinement]
```

```
## Status: ESCALATION

### Task: <task-id>
### Problem
[What's blocking]
### Epic context
[Which anti-pattern/requirement is relevant]
### Progress so far
[What's committed, what's remaining]
### Options
A. [approach] — [tradeoff]
B. [approach] — [tradeoff]
### Recommendation
[Which option and why]
```

```
## Status: CONTEXT_LIMIT

### Task: <task-id>
### Current status
[What is implemented and committed]
### Remaining
[Unchecked TaskCreate sub-steps]
### Commits so far
- [short hash]: [message]
```

**For Epic Completion Report (when all success criteria met):**
Use the COMPLETE format but replace '### Proposed next task' with:

```
### Epic Status
All success criteria met. Ready for reviewer dispatch.

### Success Criteria Evidence
- [x] Criterion 1 — [evidence: test name, file path, or command output]
- [x] Criterion 2 — [evidence]
...
```

**Change escalation protocol:**
- Instead of 'send escalation via SendMessage and wait', become 'commit partial work, write partial memory, return immediately with ESCALATION output'
- All escalation triggers remain identical (anti-pattern risk, API mismatch, rejected approach revisit, scope expansion, design flaw, consecutive TDD failures)

**Change completion protocol:**
- Instead of 'send completion report via SendMessage', become 'structure your final output as the return value'
- Memory write still happens BEFORE the return (same ordering guarantee)
- Remove 'wait for lead's response' — subagent returns, it doesn't wait

**Change context limit protocol:**
- Instead of 'send status message to lead and wait', become 'commit, write partial memory, return immediately with CONTEXT_LIMIT output'

**Change Epic Completion Report:**
- Currently at lines 282-309, uses SendMessage to team-lead
- Change to structured return format (COMPLETE variant with epic status)
- Remove 'Wait for lead to dispatch the reviewer agent' — subagent returns after outputting report

**Preserve unchanged:**
- TDD cycle (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR/COMMIT) — lines 49-120
- TaskCreate/TaskUpdate for progress tracking — lines 47, 58-60, 92-93, 99
- Project memory bridge (write learnings before returning) — lines 149-178
- bd commands (show, update, close) — lines 17-40, 123-127
- SRE refinement on proposed next task — lines 183-186
- All escalation triggers and thresholds — lines 253-276
- Escalation rules 1-6 (anti-pattern risk, API mismatch, rejected approach, scope expansion, design flaw, consecutive TDD failures)
- Handle-autonomously rules 1-4 (normal RED, refactoring, minor details, single TDD failure)
- Rules 1 (TDD mandatory), 2 (never violate anti-patterns), 4 (never skip SRE), 5 (use test-runner), 6 (TaskCreate for tracking), 7 (never close epic), 8 (memory before completion), 9 (context exhaustion protocol), 10 (commit before close)
- Rule 3 (never create next bd task without lead approval) — reframe for subagent: 'propose next task in your COMPLETE output, the lead creates it'
- Frontmatter: model: sonnet, permissionMode: bypassPermissions, memory: project, skills list

**Update frontmatter description:**
- Replace TEAMMATE/team_name language with subagent language
- Remove SendMessage examples from description
- Rewrite both examples for subagent context (dispatched via Agent tool, returns structured output)

### 2. Rewrite skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md

**Section: skill_overview (line 7)**
- 'Spawns a fresh executor teammate per task' → 'Dispatches a fresh executor subagent per task'
- 'validates every completion message' → 'validates every executor return value'

**Section: rigidity_level (line 11)**
- 'Agent lifecycle is native (Claude Code manages teams, executors idle out naturally)' → 'Agent lifecycle is blocking (lead waits for each executor subagent to return)'

**Section: quick_reference (lines 17-27)**
- 'Agent tool with team_name and name params' → 'Agent tool (blocking subagent, no team_name)'
- Remove 'Critical: Old executor idles out naturally'
- Add note: 'Critical: Agent call blocks until executor returns — lead stays actively computing (no idle throttling)'

**Section: 2. Spawn Executor (lines 65-96)**
- Remove entire paragraph about MUST be spawned as teammate (lines 67-68)
- Replace spawn template with new subagent template:

```
Agent tool:
  subagent_type: "hyperpowers:executor"
  mode: "bypassPermissions"
  prompt: |
    You are the executor agent. Execute task <task-id> in epic <epic-id>.

    Your scope is task <task-id> ONLY. After completing it, output a
    structured completion report (Status: COMPLETE) as your final response.

    Start with: bd show <epic-id>
    Then: bd show <task-id>

    Important context:
    - Read project memories matching epic-<epic-id>-* for cross-task
      learnings from prior tasks.
    - Use TaskCreate to track your TDD sub-steps (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR/COMMIT).
    - Before outputting your completion report, write key learnings to
      project memory using filename: epic-<epic-id>-task-<task-id>-learnings.md
    - If you hit an escalation trigger, commit partial work, write partial
      memory, and return immediately with Status: ESCALATION output.
```

- Remove name collision workaround ('executor-2') — no names needed for subagents
- Remove team_name parameter entirely

**Section: 3. Validate Proposals (lines 98-168)**

**3a. On Task Completion Report (lines 100-131):**
- 'Executor sends completion reports' → 'Executor returns structured output as Agent tool result'
- Add status parsing: 'Parse the ## Status: header. Handle COMPLETE, ESCALATION, or CONTEXT_LIMIT'
- Keep 5-item validation checklist exactly as-is (lines 118-122)
- Keep verify-commits-first step (line 114)
- APPROVE/MODIFY/REDIRECT: 'Dispatch fresh subagent' instead of 'Spawn fresh executor'

**3b. On Escalation (lines 133-141):**
- Retitle: 'Handling ESCALATION Return'
- Steps 1-4 stay identical (re-read epic, check Approaches Considered, check revisit conditions, ask USER)
- Replace step 5 ('SendMessage to executor with decision') with:
  'Dispatch fresh executor subagent for the SAME task with decision in prompt:
  Prompt addition: Prior executor escalated: [problem]. Lead decision: [decision]. Prior commits: [hashes]. Read project memory for partial learnings.'

**3c. On Progress Message (lines 143-145):**
- Remove entirely. Subagents use TaskCreate/TaskUpdate which are visible natively. No SendMessage progress possible.

**Add new section: 3d. Handling CONTEXT_LIMIT Return:**
- Parse the checkpoint info from executor output
- Dispatch fresh executor subagent for the SAME task with checkpoint context in prompt:
  'Prompt addition: Prior executor hit context limit. Progress: [committed work]. Remaining: [unchecked sub-steps]. Read project memory for partial learnings.'

**Section: 4. Completion Gate (lines 148-168)**
- GAPS FOUND handling: Replace 'Send gap details to executor via SendMessage' with:
  'Dispatch fresh executor subagent for SAME task with gap details in prompt:
  Prompt addition: Reviewer found gaps. Fix these items: [gap list]. Prior commits: [hashes]. Task is already closed in bd — reopen with bd update <task-id> --status in_progress before fixing.'
- Note: When reviewer finds gaps, task may already be closed. Re-dispatch prompt must include instruction to reopen task before fixing.
- Keep reviewer dispatch as subagent (unchanged)

**Section: critical_rules (lines 220-249)**
- Remove rule 6 ('Reviewer is a subagent, not a teammate')
- Remove rule 9 ('Team-based dispatch is mandatory')
- Remove rationalization: 'I'll just use the Agent tool directly, it's simpler'
- Add new rule: 'Executor MUST be dispatched as blocking subagent (no team_name) — teammate dispatch causes idle throttling that stalls unattended execution'
- Keep rules: 1 (lead never implements), 2 (validate every proposal), 3 (check Design Discovery), 4 (executor per task), 5 (memory bridge mandatory), 7 (epic requirements immutable), 8 (one epic at a time)
- Rule 4 update: 'Fresh context per task. Each subagent returns, lead dispatches next.' Remove 'idles out naturally'

**Section: examples (lines 183-217)**
- Completion report example: Show parsing Agent tool return value, not receiving SendMessage
- Escalation example: Show return-and-redispatch pattern, not SendMessage exchange

**Section: integration (lines 275-291)**
- 'agents/executor.md (teammate, team_name, fresh per task)' → 'agents/executor.md (subagent, blocking, fresh per task)'
- Remove 'Executor not responding → Check git/bd for prior progress, spawn fresh executor' (subagent either returns or times out; if timeout, re-dispatch for same task)
- Update 'Reviewer GAPS → SendMessage gap list' → 'Reviewer GAPS → re-dispatch executor subagent with gap list in prompt'
- Add: 'Executor timeout → re-dispatch for same task with checkpoint context if available'

## Success Criteria
- [ ] agents/executor.md has zero SendMessage references
- [ ] agents/executor.md has zero 'team-lead' references
- [ ] agents/executor.md has zero 'wait for lead' references
- [ ] agents/executor.md has zero 'idles out' references
- [ ] agents/executor.md defines three return formats (COMPLETE, ESCALATION, CONTEXT_LIMIT)
- [ ] agents/executor.md Epic Completion Report section updated to return format (no SendMessage)
- [ ] agents/executor.md frontmatter description updated for subagent context
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md dispatches executor without team_name
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md has zero SendMessage references
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md has zero 'team-lead' references
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md has zero 'idles out' references
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md has zero 'teammate' references
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md handles ESCALATION return with re-dispatch pattern
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md handles CONTEXT_LIMIT return with re-dispatch pattern
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md handles GAPS FOUND with re-dispatch (including task reopen instruction)
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md preserves 5-item validation checklist
- [ ] executing-plans/SKILL.md preserves Design Discovery checks
- [ ] Both files preserve project memory bridge protocol
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Verification Commands
Run these after implementation to verify all criteria:
```bash
# Zero forbidden terms in executor agent
rg -c 'SendMessage' agents/executor.md        # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'team-lead' agents/executor.md          # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'wait for.*lead' agents/executor.md     # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'idles? out' agents/executor.md         # expect: 0 or no output

# Zero forbidden terms in executing-plans
rg -c 'SendMessage' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md   # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'team-lead' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md     # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'idles? out' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md    # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'teammate' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md      # expect: 0 or no output
rg -c 'team_name' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md     # expect: 0 or no output

# Positive checks — new patterns present
rg 'Status: COMPLETE' agents/executor.md               # expect: match
rg 'Status: ESCALATION' agents/executor.md             # expect: match
rg 'Status: CONTEXT_LIMIT' agents/executor.md          # expect: match
rg 'subagent_type.*executor' skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md  # expect: match
```

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Subagent Timeout**
- What if executor subagent times out (Agent tool returns timeout error)?
- Lead MUST handle this by re-dispatching for the same task with checkpoint context
- Add to executing-plans integration section under 'When stuck'
- Prompt addition: 'Prior executor timed out. Check git log and bd show for progress. Continue from where they left off.'

**Edge Case: GAPS FOUND Re-dispatch with Closed Task**
- When reviewer finds gaps, the executor has already closed the bd task
- The re-dispatched executor needs to reopen the task before fixing
- Include explicit instruction in re-dispatch prompt: 'Task is already closed — run bd update <task-id> --status in_progress before fixing gaps'

**Edge Case: Malformed Executor Return**
- Executor might not follow the Status format perfectly (especially under context pressure)
- Lead should look for Status header; if not found, treat as COMPLETE and validate normally
- Don't need explicit error handling — just parse what's there

**Clarification: name Parameter**
- The 'name' parameter in Agent tool is for teammate addressing (SendMessage)
- Subagents don't need it — remove from spawn template
- Only subagent_type, mode, and prompt are needed

**Clarification: Epic Completion Report**
- Executor currently has a separate Epic Completion Report section (lines 282-309) that uses SendMessage
- This MUST be updated to use the COMPLETE return format variant with Epic Status section
- Remove 'Wait for lead to dispatch reviewer' — subagent just returns

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO team_name parameter (causes idle throttling)
- ❌ NO SendMessage in executor (subagents return output, not send messages)
- ❌ NO 'wait for lead' patterns (subagents return, they don't wait)
- ❌ NO 'idles out' language (subagents return, they don't idle)
- ❌ NO placeholder text in either file (all sections must be complete)
- ❌ NO changes to TDD cycle, project memory format, or TaskCreate usage

title: Update brainstorming skill with architecture node auto-detection
design:
## Goal
Update skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md to auto-detect architecture node references and load their context (volatility axis, interface contract, relevant ADRs, adjacent nodes) during Step 1, presenting this as context for the architect to curate during the brainstorm.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (targeted update to existing file, not new skill creation)

## Implementation

### 1. Read current brainstorming skill
- skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md — the file to modify
- Identify insertion point: Step 1 (Understanding the Idea), after 'Check current state'

### 2. Add architecture node detection sub-section to Step 1

After the existing 'Check current state' block, add architecture node detection:

a) Check for architecture graph:
   bd list --label arch --type epic --status open

b) If graph exists, check if user's request references an architecture node:
   - By bd ID mention (user says 'bd-xxx')
   - By component name mention (user mentions a name matching a node title)
   - By explicit mention ('the payment component', 'the pricing engine')

c) If arch node detected, load context:
   - bd show <node-id> — get volatility axis, layer, interface contract, responsibility
   - bd dep tree <node-id> — get adjacent nodes (blocks, relates_to)
   - ls doc/arch/adr-*.md — find related ADRs
   - Read ADRs that mention the node or its volatility axis

d) Present loaded context (NOT auto-insert as anti-patterns):
   ```
   Architecture context loaded for [node name]:
   - Volatility axis: [from node design]
   - Layer: [from node design]
   - Interface contract: [from node design]
   - Adjacent nodes: [from bd deps]
   - Relevant ADRs:
     - ADR-NNN: [title] — [key decision and consequences]

   These ADRs represent architectural decisions that may inform
   anti-patterns for this epic.
   ```

e) Continue with normal brainstorming flow. Architect curates which
   tradeoffs become anti-patterns during the Socratic dialogue.

### 3. Handle edge cases

- No architecture graph exists: detection finds nothing, proceed normally
- Multiple nodes referenced: load context for all
- Node is closed/stable: still load context (historical design decisions)
- Node has no ADRs: note 'No ADRs found for this component'
- User doesn't reference any node: skip detection, proceed normally

### 4. Update integration section

Add reference to volatility-decomposition and architectural-audit skills
in the integration section of brainstorming.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Step 1 includes architecture node detection sub-section
- [ ] Detection checks for bd ID, component name, and explicit mention
- [ ] Context loading includes: volatility axis, layer, interface contract, adjacent nodes, ADRs
- [ ] ADRs presented as context, NOT auto-inserted as anti-patterns
- [ ] Edge cases handled (no graph, multiple nodes, closed nodes, no ADRs)
- [ ] Integration section references outer-loop skills
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns
- NO auto-inserting ADR tradeoffs as epic anti-patterns (architect curates)
- NO modifying the existing brainstorming process steps (add, don't change)
- NO breaking existing brainstorming functionality

title: Revert wave/team execution system
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Remove all wave-planning and team-executing-plans skill files
- Remove execute-wave command
- Remove Parallelism Map from brainstorming epic template and all wave-related content
- Restore simple brainstorming → writing-plans → executing-plans flow in using-hyper
- Keep agent definition improvements (memory, permissionMode, disallowedTools) from commit 1460ce5
- Bump version to 2.10.0

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] skills/wave-planning/ directory deleted
- [ ] skills/team-executing-plans/ directory deleted
- [ ] commands/execute-wave.md deleted
- [ ] brainstorming/SKILL.md has no Parallelism Map, no team vs solo decision, no team path handoff
- [ ] using-hyper/SKILL.md has simple linear flow (brainstorming → writing-plans → executing-plans)
- [ ] Agent files (code-reviewer, codebase-investigator, internet-researcher, test-runner) retain their memory/permissionMode/disallowedTools improvements
- [ ] plugin.json version is 2.10.0
- [ ] No references to wave-planning, team-executing-plans, or Parallelism Map remain in modified files
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO reverting agent definition improvements (they are independently useful and unrelated to waves)
- NO git revert or git reset (surgical edits are cleaner than mechanical reverts for mixed commits)
- NO leaving dangling references to waves/Parallelism Map in brainstorming or using-hyper
- NO modifying skills that weren't touched by the wave commits (writing-plans, executing-plans, etc.)

## Approach
Surgically remove wave-related content while preserving the independently valuable agent improvements. Delete 3 files entirely, edit brainstorming and using-hyper to restore pre-wave state, bump version.

## Architecture
- Delete: skills/wave-planning/, skills/team-executing-plans/, commands/execute-wave.md
- Edit: skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md (remove ~172 lines of wave content)
- Edit: skills/using-hyper/SKILL.md (restore ~24 lines to pre-wave routing)
- Edit: .claude-plugin/plugin.json (version 2.9.0 -> 2.10.0)
- Keep: agents/*.md (all improvements retained)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The wave/team execution system (5 commits, v2.9.0) does not match actual usage patterns. Work is mostly sequential, rarely has 3+ independent streams. When team execution is attempted, multiple agents struggle to work on the same repo simultaneously. The added ceremony (Parallelism Map, wave-planning, team-executing-plans) provides no value for the dominant use case.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Surgical revert (keep agent improvements) - CHOSEN
Surgical editing is cleaner than git revert for commits that bundled unrelated changes. Agent improvements are independently valuable.
